When we first started dating we were friends of 6 years and in those first weeks of our actual relationship he was so nice to me, I’d never seen that side of him. Eventually he became really mean to me. We lived together since before our relationship started, so I was always around him and didn’t really have a way to get some alone time. He would insult my intelligence and my personality, never my looks. It hurts a lot when someone you like so much is insulting your actual being and self. Because I can change my appearance to an extent, but my personality isn’t something I can just alter. It really hurt and I carried it on for the next year. After we got our apartment, he got much nicer to me thought we had some ups and downs. Out of nowhere he started to hate me again, and said something I’m literally never going to forget “I can’t wait for you to leave so I can live my life” and then he confirmed that he meant he wanted to hoe around and see other people. He apologized, not very well, but he apologized and after we talked about it he got insanely nice and I’d never had him be so good to me. I finally wasn’t feeling like an absolute waste of space and actually started to open up to him more and more and got comfortable with him. In the last few days, I don’t feel important to him. When he’s not working, he’s on his phone or playing video games with his friends and all I hear is him sounding so much happier to talk to them than he ever has been to talk to me. It’s really obvious and he has confirmed verbally that I am nowhere near the most important thing to him, nor am I even top 3. But he is the most important thing to me. I have no friends and my family and I don’t get along. So when he’s busy, when he’s having fun or doing his own thing, wether it’s out with friends or just at home playing games, I have absolutely nothing to do and nobody to talk to. I don’t have anything going for me other than work. I don’t have any interest in any hobbies, my coworkers don’t like me and are all atleast 4x my age. (I’m 17 and they’re all over their 60’s). I often hear them talking bad about me, or they have no interest in actually talking to me and have told my boss that I bother them when I do try to take some time to interact with them(maybe I’m excessive, and that’s my bad). I feel so lonely, so stupid, like a bad person and like there’s nothing going for me ever. I want friends to hang out with and family to talk to, I want my boyfriend to do things with me and have fun being around me. Nobody is ever happy when they’re around me. Why does nobody like me? So, I thought that this 7th grade year I was going to change my life around. But I ended up in the same place I was back in sixth grade. I hated sixth grade. I had a lot of arguments with my mom. I lost my best friend. I lost almost all my friends. The one thing I hated the most was, I got fat. I didn’t even notice I was getting fat. I realized that a lot of people were mad at me for who I became at the end of the year from who I was at the beginning. I realized no one wanted to hang out with me. That made me feel lonely. Nobody knows who you are on the inside anymore. Its all based on the little actions you make. That’s how society works. They determine how you are doing or how you are feeling by one little action. Suddenly, I’m begging for attention and I’m a pick me. I only wanted people to understand how I was feeling. I don’t like it when people tell me it’s going to be okay, or I’ll find peace when I know I’m not. I don’t want to be known as “The girl who begs for attention”. I wanted to change my life around. I wanted to have a “glow up”, but I knew I would never achieve wanting to be pretty. My parents told me that I was going to an “Institution of learning”, and I didn’t need to feel pretty. 7th grade must be the worst year of my life. Life has no surprises anymore. Its to the point where I overthink and end up knowing what’s going to happen. I overthought the fact that no one would understand, and I was right. People will never understand. The only person I could probably talk to without them feeling some type of way is my aunt. My aunt has been through it so she understands what im going through.

I know they dont want to buy me a car. Ive known for a while and thats not the issue. ill just buy my own car, but my mother won't allow me to work during the school year because she thinks itll bring my grades down. my gpa is a 93 right now. though my sisters grades were higher in highschool. Recently I was diagnosed with T1D (diabetes type 1) which is passed down through the men in my family. So now Im an EXPENSIVE kid they dont want. Ive always been a little aware that my sister was a favorite because theres shelves upon shelves of pictures of her as a baby and Ive rummaged through the entire house only to find TWO pictures of me. when I asked about this my mother said "the first child is just more exciting". At dinner while my sister was living at home, she would take priority. I spent most of the time sitting there listening or zoning out as they never asked me what was going on in my life. This caused a bit of a culture shock once my sister left for college and I was the only kid at the dinner table. They would act like I was making things up about myself that ive always been super open about, like my fear of the ocean which caused me to refuse to take showers as a kid because I thought the ground would burst open as a megalodon swallowed me from below. for the past two birthdays they have forgotten about it and gotten something last minute. for my most recent 17th birthday (my sister got her car at this point) I got toe nail clippers!! yeah, it was weird. He ended up telling me it was my fault that I dont ever tell them anything, which is a straight up lie. I have very obvious special interests, and I talk all the time. He just forgets everything I tell him. My dad has increasingly been very mean to me. One day I burped at the table and he yelled at me, so I reminded him that he literally burps in my mothers face and he told me to get out of his house. I walked around the neighborhood until my mother came and got me. One day while walking the dog, my mother came with me to break the news that my father LITERALLY HATES ME. yeah, straight up. and that he had for years. She said it was because I dont just sit there and oblige, basically I dont kiss his ass. But lately thats all Ive been trying to do. I keep my answers short and I never look him in the eye anymore. it hasnt done anything. He still treats me like im his enemy. Yesterday a film that I wrote the script for that I was very proud of premiered at my school. My dad REFUSED to go to the premiere. Today, ironically, my sisters film for college premiered. it was a scheduled livestream and he set aside time to watch the entire thing, sitting through other peoples films as well. I was in the other room as my mother spoke FOR me which she does a lot, which causes him to hate me more a lot of the time. She told him that i was upset at him for not watching my film but watching my sisters, and he said its different because I didnt work as hard. and when my mother told him about how hard I worked, his next excuse was that IVE HAD TROUBLE GETTING MYSELF OUT OF BED FOR A MONTH???? theres been a film premiere every year so far, he hasnt attended a single one. he has seen none of my work, and this goes beyond just the films. he is so incredibly disinterested in my life, all he cares about is making me feel miserable and I cant figure out why.

it's a long story.
for context -  this is all at school.

more context - everyone for some reason always tells me about everything going on in their lives. when they do this, my mind instantly goes to "how can i fix this" mode, and most of the time, i can't, which just sends my mood spiraling down, down, down. might make them feel better, but makes me more stressed.
and a teensy bit more context - i'm non-binary.
so let's get into it! (*nervous laughter*)

so, during a fire drill, my friend fell on her arm, the same one she broke last year. it went numb and  she couldn't move it.

then, during recess, a friend comes up to me and starts talking about how he is nervous about crushes and he wants to ask this girl out but he thinks someone  else has better chances than he does, which makes him feel terrible. as i said before, i feel like i can't do anything, which just stresses me out further, but i make the unwise decision to say nothing and just let his problems sink in. then -- he says he's jealous of me, because apparently i have it easier. i don't have to worry about crushes. except i kind of do, maybe even more so, because if i get a crush they have to be ok with dating a non-binary person, which lots aren't.
so yeah.

then, this girl decides  to overhear our convo and start yelling at him about it. i, being a good friend, tell her to stop, and then she delivers the two lines that shattered me.

"why are you even getting involved? it's not like you have any problems or anything."

Im 21, female... and i cannot express to you how lonely i actually am. Its a sad, unescapable feeling that swallows me. The days are fine, but the nights get me. I feel pathetic, honestly. I don't hate myself.. but i most certainly don't take care of myself as i know i likely should. i don't SOCIALIZE as i know i should. I LONG for, and CRAVE cuddles, tickles (yes, tickles), affection.. love. It feels so far out of my reach, its indescribably painful. Like im from the outside looking in. I never got this way as a teenager.. i liked to live out a fantasy but felt uncomfortable commiting to a relationship. embarrassed to, cause i didn't understand it. but now... now i understand, and being a loner in highschool didn't help me. isolating from everyone sabotaged me. I Crave dark things out of this loneliness. Yanderes, Tsunderes... I crave love at first sight something to just... immediately extuinguish this hurtful, painful longing in my chest that pours out of my eyes. I feel ugly, sorry for myself. I think about what i could of done better, how a man (who would want a serious relationship, Christian family values, and kids) could ever WANT me. Want my personality, want to wake up next to me.. want to even talk to me without seeing me as awkward, or.. not worth it. In a way i blame my mother for programming this into me...

but i don't at the same time. ... She's just as sick as i am, so how could she know what is right, and what is wrong? If she's accepting her mental illness. I don't want to be that... but im slowly slipping into that taught worthlessness.

Hey, no one might ever read this, but I need to let something off my chest. I’ve kept it in, and honestly, it’s like a nasty fart—if I don’t let it out, it’ll kill me.

Imagine this: You’re just trying to live your life. You’re normal—wear glasses, have smile lines, nothing out of the ordinary. Now imagine getting called ugly by random strangers. When you ignore them, they get angry, start spreading rumors, and won’t leave you alone.

For a while, I worked at Borough Market in London, a busy place with lots of cameras. I was minding my business, doing my job, but people kept picking on me for something I can’t control: my face. At first, the insults hurt. They’d call me ugly over and over, like they didn’t have anything else in their vocabulary. But over time, it stopped hurting and just became jarring.

Now let me be real: I’m fat, and that’s on me. I’ve been lazy, focusing on making money and getting through uni. But the comments weren’t even about that. It was always the same thing: ugly, ugly, ugly. It wasn’t even creative, and honestly, it bugged me more because of how basic it was. Like, if I’m gonna be bullied, at least let the bullies be smart enough to come up with better insults, right?

Here’s the kicker—I didn’t even grow up here. I was born in nigeria, and even my English felt more advanced than theirs. That was the embarrassing part, not their insults but the fact that my bullies were...well, not the brightest. I started taunting them back, and yeah, maybe I shouldn’t have, but they came into my life to annoy the hell out of me, so I thought, why not believe in karma?

The company eventually let me go, saying I didn’t meet their customer service standards. Fine, that’s their choice. But deep down, I knew it wasn’t about that. It was just a toxic environment. The worst part wasn’t even the firing—it was realizing how petty and judgmental people can be.

Fast forward to now: I have a decent job as a consultant, and I’m doing okay. But I’m still considering starting a YouTube channel. I mean, why not? Let’s see how “ugly” I really am, right? Because honestly, after what I’ve been through, YouTube comments won’t scare me.

Yeah, I might be fat, black, a woman, and “ugly,” but let me tell you something: I am a child of God, and only HE can judge me.

I feel really bad about this vent, because I know it makes me sound like a pompous, privileged person who doesn’t have right to complain, cause I am. There are other people struggling. REALLY struggling, with all sorts of problems. So many people can’t afford to live, and I’m upset, because I don’t know what to ask for Christmas. My parents don’t really like My lists, and I really don’t want to dissapoint them again. I’m 19, and ever since I’ve started getting older, my parents have been having some constructive criticism about my Christmas/birthday wishlists. They say that they’re childish, and immature, and they want me to ask for more practical things. Problem is, I don’t want a lot of practical things. And then they disapprove of the practical things that I do want. A couple years ago, I wanted a trash bin for my bedroom, and then they said they weren’t getting me a trash bin for Christmas. They got me a trash bin anyway, and it was really sweet of them, but it wasn’t for Christmas, and that was a practical thing that I couldn’t ask for Christmas, even though they told me to ask for more practical things. I’m also very happy with my life, and there’s not many things that I want, so my lists aren’t that long. But then my parents are upset again because my list isn’t long enough. So then I have to add more things to it. I hate asking for things. I don’t like to inconvenience people, and my parents do so much for me. I love them so much, and I don’t feel like I should be asking them for things. I shouldn’t be asking more from them. There’s also not many things that I want, so coming up with new things is really stressful, because I end up just asking for things that I like, and would like to have, but don’t really care about them, and the end up being kind of small, insignificant things, and then my parents get upset, because my list ends up just being merch for fandoms that I’m in, and then the list just ends up being childish again, and I’m back to square one. But the things that I really want, are really expensive, so I feel bad for asking for them, especially when my parents do so much for me. Usually, it’s broadway tickets. I always want tickets to broadway. But 2 tickets alone pretty much takes up my whole budget, and that doesn’t include travel, and souvenirs, and food. This year, I really want to see the Outsiders, although I would be thrilled to go see any musical on broadway right now. Outsiders is just my top pick. But my parents also just bought me a car for college, and they told be to be concious of that when making my list. So even though they never said that I can’t ask for broadway tickets, I feel like I can’t ask for broadway tickets. I don’t want to ask too much of them. I usually get away with my Christmas lists by asking for books. They’re grown up enough and practical enough to not be childish, and there’s usually enough of them that I want to fill up my list. I love to read, and I like getting and buying books. But my parents are tired of my lists being 90% books. And now that I work at a Library, my parents have had a conversation with me, telling me to not ask for a bunch of books this year, since I can just get my books at the library. But 1, this isn’t always the case. A lot of the books I want aren’t at the library. And 2, despite loving my job, and working at a library, I hate checking out books at the library. It makes me really anxious. I consider myself a fast reader, but it still takes me a little bit to finish a book. I don’t like deadlines, and it makes reading feel like a chore, so I lose motivation to read, and then I don’t finish the book, and then I’m upset because I didn’t get to finish it. I really don’t want to put in the effort to borrow a book that I’m not going to get to finish. I’m also not a very organized person, and there’s a big chance I’m going to lose the book anyway. There’s so much pressure with library books. I would rather just buy the books, and have Them to read in my own time, whenever I want. But now my parents are telling me to not ask for a bunch of books, and now I have no idea what to ask for. I can’t ask for books, I can’t ask for the things I really want, and if I just fill my wishlist with a bunch of useless merch, it’s too childish. But I really don’t know what else to ask for! I've been thinking about what happened to me tonight. I know I've thought about it before. Other nights. Today is one of those nights.

I think the question that mostly comes to my mind in these nights is 'How serious it was?' Too much? Not so much? And I try to answer those questions and I don't know. 

A teacher told me, a few days ago that he admired me. For the conditions I went through, and my dedication with my studies. And it felt great, at the moment. But it made me remember. And I was so blissfully ignorant this few months, that I almost forgot entirely. But I remember.

Tonight, I didn't brush my teeth. It should feel gross. I can't help but think about how I'm used to it now. How annoying it's to brush it so frequently. 

Maybe it was too much? Too serious, something that broke me entirely? But I don't know what I am. I'm not broken, I think. I function mostly fine. But I wonder if mostly fine is a problem. If I should be fine. Perhaps I should be more broken to be truly broken. How broken is that? Should I use a numeric scale? I don't know. 

I talk coherently. I behave fine. I'm not that unhealthy. But in nights like these, I wonder what happened to me. Perhaps what I went through implanted something inside me. Something that damages me from the inside. But it's surprising. I can cry from what happened but it seems that it didn't take any effect on me. Perhaps nothing happened at all.

I don't know. I should be sleeping. But I wonder, again, what I am. If I'm whole. I don't think I truly know what whole feels. But I'm thinking whole doesn't feel like I feel now.

A whole lot is going wrong in my life right now, and I've been struggling with depression and anxiety for a while. This really close friend of mine also is going through similar things to me, and she always comes to me to vent or look for advice, that I gladly give her. I really love her, and I appreciate her so much. 

But my little brother passed away last december, I'm stressed about college, and certain stuff concerning my parents, while also dealing with lots of self confidence and self esteem issues. So, I went to my friend and vented about all of this via chat. Especially the self confidence part, because I don't feel confident in my intellect or abilities in general as much as I did before, and everyone around me agrees that I'm a smart girl and such, but I've found it hard to see it that way for a few months now.

 I'm really anxious about college and getting a job, and all that grown up stuff. And my friend didn't really know what to say to me, which fine, not everyone is good at comforting people, she's probably feeling the same distress as me about the grown up stuff too, and I'm happy she took her time to listen anyways, but when I started telling her how insecure I am regarding my intellect and how I couldn't reach the goals that I set last year (when I was in a wayy better state of mine, and actually thought I could accomplish them), she told me I was just "making excuses". 

And she's not my therapist, she's not to blame for me taking that as offensive, but I also find it really tone-deaf, insensitive and antipathetic how she just completely disregarded my feelings and went "you're just making excuses", like... Don't you think I've been TRYING? I've been trying HARD to not have a breakdown since my LITERAL KID BROTHER DIED LAST YEAR, and on top of that, I also have to deal with my dad and his horrible decisions that constantly affect me.

She knows, she'd BEEN knowing since months ago how bad my mental health got when summer vacation started, and it's mid-August right now. And she also knows I've been trying hard to do what I can. So how could she just ignore everything I said and act like I'm not doing enough? 

When my brother died I managed it the best way one could manage something like that. And then everything got out of control, but I stayed composed, I didn't let it get through my head, I didn't talk about it more that necessary. I'm always letting disrespect slide, always being a good girl, a good student, a good daughter, I break my back at school, I take my future very seriously, and that's exactly why I'm so anxious about it, I don't want to take bad decisions and I try to plan it throughoutly, to always have an answer, a solution. And she knows that. 

The fact that she took my problems, my emotions and insecurities, and made it seem like I was "making excuses" because I feel anxious and not enough isn't the main problem. I know that. She isn't the one to blame for anything bad that happened to me since last year. But, the fact that she doesn't even want to understand my struggles, to comfort me, and instead chose to make it seem like it's my fault things didn't go as planned is certainly a CHOICE. Especially when I ALWAYS listen to her and try to see where she's coming from, and I ALWAYS help her get through her own problems and insecurities. 

I just feel so betrayed, so unwanted. And maybe I'm the problem, but I can't get over the fact that she is seeing that I genuinely believe I'm not good enough for anything that I wish for, and her way of making me feel better it's just saying "You're making excuses"

And this has happened before. I talk to someone about my problems, they start saying I'm strong and smart and whatever, and when I express any type of insecurity they just go "what? Do you feel insecure? YOU?" 

And I would take it as a compliment, if it wasn't for the fact that they seem to forget I'm a person with feelings who also struggles like anybody else, and just because I'm not actively showing it on the surface, doesn't mean I'm not affected or that I'm not trying to deal with it.

Idk how this website works, I just needed to feel heard. But if somebody takes the time to read this and thinks I'm in the wrong, and it's possible to answer vents or smt, I will gladly hear why I'm wrong and what I could do to improve :)

It’s been three years now, and I’m almost 20 and in college, and it never really stopped. Sometimes it’s better, sometimes worse. But I never felt normal again. I have the urge to cut myself constantly. All I have to do is get out of bed and walk a few inches away. Atop a shelf holds those metal blades that grace me with the freedom from my sorrow for a few minutes before dread washes over me. Dizziness takes over and I lay there in a daze before coming back to my senses and cleaning up the crime scene on my body. I lie in my bed worriedly pondering over how I’m meant to hide them from the world until healed enough to look old. I wish this cycle would end but nothing else seemed to substitute the release I get from these painful marks on my fragile skin that are a constant reminder of my suffering.

I wish my family loved me. Really, truly loved me. I can’t say I had a god awful childhood, I was financially privileged. Not rich by any means, but upper middle class. Enough to attend a private school on financial aid and take an out of state vacation once in a while. Enough for my family to buy me that movie I wanted or stop for a treat after school. I have no father, so I was raised by my mom and grandma. Both were emotionally abusive, my mother was physically abusive. Yet, somehow, I lived with it and I still loved them. It all changed when I was 11. I’m transgender, my whole family are Oklahoma Baptists. At first, my mom was accepting. At least she seemed to act like it. It was my anchor. I never wanted to accept she was a bad person, because she’d be the only one left after I transitioned. My entire rest of my family is extremely homophobic and see trans people like some disease. I’ve struggled with self-harm and suicidal ideation my whole life, and every time I was the one who pulled myself out of it, I was never allowed to have a therapist. Now, I’m 18, and finally getting a serious look at HRT. Now my mom is gone too. Only emotionally. She’s still supporting me financially, which I am eternally grateful for, but it’s becoming more and more apparent she does not and will never approve of my “decision”. I have nobody in my family to really connect with. They do things for me monetarily to make up for lacking in every other area. I just want to be loved for who and what I am. I know I never will be, they aren’t the sort of people to change their minds, but one can dream I suppose.

Dear, “Dad”,
I hate that I still grieve over you. Even if you’re alive, it feels like you’re dead.
I don’t even know why I care this much. I really wish I could just forget about you
like how you forget about me. Well? Was it worth it?
You didn’t get mom back.
You saw her move on to a better life.
So why did you drag me back from healing?
Why did you keep hurting me if you knew there was no outcome? 
It was so you could prove mom that you could be a “good dad”, right?
It was all about how you looked on the outside.
But it was all lies. All of it.

I kept the abuse and neglect a secret from mom for too many years.
And when I spoke up about it I didn’t get an apology.
All I got was silence.
I felt like I did a terrible thing, and that I had to beg for your forgiveness. 
As if I did something wrong. That moment when it all spilled out was the scariest moment of my life.
It was worse than getting hit.
Worse than being left alone. 
I guess you didn’t like that I exposed you. Now you would never have the non-existent chance to get back with mom.
Aw.
I know why you abused me. It’s because you liked the power. You liked the imbalance.
The manipulations and pretend love.
I kept falling into your traps

Over and over and over.

I kept falling for your love bombs. I’ve done it so much I started to pick up the habit and become you.
I started love bombing my own mom. Can’t imagine how that felt for her.
I mean, all she can see in me is you right?
That’s all I see too.
All I am is you. We’re each other. And I hate it so much.

The last time I remember seeing you
was when you took a picture of yourself in the hospital bed after the car crash.
“Bring me water. I’m so thirsty.”
But once you heard my mom came to pick me up, you changed.
You didn’t want me. You asked for her. Do you not understand how painful that felt for me?
I felt like crap.
It made me realize I was just a place holder. A broken puzzle piece that somehow fit between you two.
I was the parent in the relationship. I was the responsible one for you. Why couldn’t you just be my dad?
I wish you were never in my life in the first place.
That would have been better than this.


Yours biologically,
Kaleiah.

The thing is, I forced myself to love civil engineering during my 1st year since I had no choice and my parents wanted me to so I'm having a hard time choosing since I already started but I would sacrifice some of the subjects I already took, which I had good grades in them and focuses more on civil engineering. I saw my future and how I wanted to be as a civil engineer after I graduated, I had set plans for myself, and I'm also interested in building since I like designing houses, not thinking about the journey I would face to get to the goals I have made. Compared to computer engineering I have experiences that I also enjoyed during my high school days such as HTML, java, CSS, and c++, but c++ was recent during my 1st year as a civil student and I grew fond of it. I can set my new goals in life again with this course but I'm having a hard time and need to adjust again if I'm going to shift since it's been almost a year that I have stuck with my previous goals and ideas in life that I was fond of. So I thought if I'm struggling during my 1st year in the subject that is important to civil engineering twice, then it may be not for me.

I always get attacked for liking Trump and stuff, online or in person, anywhere. Worst part is that it's by other queer people. Straight people accept me, but then the lgbt people, the people that are supposed to support me are the ones who hate me?? I don't even try to be loud about my views, I mention a little thing when it comes up, but I hate politic talk. So i don't know why they do it. It really hurts. Makes me feel like I'm not a real gay person sometimes. I don't even like to associate with the community anymore because of it. I can't find a single queer person who can look past politics. It makes me feel really guilty for some reason.

He’s still with me as a spirit ex, but there are just some things I can only express in different forms of communication outside of what is normal communication. I feel like I’m not as spiritual, but I realize that I have somewhat of a connection to the Claires like clairvoyant for example. I also coming to terms of my neurodivergence like autism, which also been a source of pain, aside from my family & life. I looked at the Music movie by Sia. It was a controversial movie & I agree, but in a way I could relate to Music where she was in her own world with the music, but here sister who I suspected to be autistic and even adhd as well just by how she functions, I can relate to based on wanting to be a free sprit all while having a world or worlds like Music but in a sense of a paracosm. I can function like a human, but can’t fully & it shows. I struggle to get a diagnosis for autism/adhd bc there so few doctors in Mississippi, but also they tend to prioritize children even tho the government could give less of a shit about the poor. I cried a bit bc it hurts that I struggle to get my life going, but David is so encouraging. He really has helped me out, but I feel like there is stuff that I can only work through & I’m not in a place clean & clear. 

I feel like it’s word salad here & I have so much more to talk about, but I feel tired. I appreciate the talk. Goodnight.

I went to a christmasbparty last night and hung out with my anti-social friend for most of it cause I haven't seen her in a while. I was mainly just listening to my friend vent and talk, but eventually (after about an hour from the original time we got there) I could tell my mom wanted me out there, so I told my friend that I was going to join the party and that she could come if she wanted, my friend poltely told me no, so I went and joined my family. She (my mom) then texted me "you understand why I'm upset?" And I did, because I knew she wanted me to join the party. I told her this, and also that that was why I cam out here to join thr party. Later she told me that she was angry at my friend when I asked what was wrong. I kind of understand, but I also just wanted to hang out with my friend. My mom was right though, if my friend hadn't been there then I would have spent the party hanging out with my family.

My parents are always persisting and pressuring me to do good in school, so when I get a C or B, they always tell me it's not good enough. I'm forced to wake up early in the morning to help my parents at their café and afterschool, I'm forced to help till late at night. Sometimes, I have to wake up extra early because of a school or sporting event. When exams are getting closer and assignment are almost due, my parents, even though they want me to do good in school, still force me to help them at work which means I have less time to study or do assignments. When this happens, I force myself to stay up late at night to study or do assignments without my parents knowing. I've stayed up for so many nights that at school, I fall asleep in class or stop paying attention because I tend to zone out a lot. My laptop is also broken so I use my sister's, and we don't have any Wi-Fi access at home so I have to hotspot my phone to the laptop. Sometimes though, my phone gets taken away when I'm helping at the café so I can't hotspot to do my work. I have all of these problems yet my parents are blaming me for not focusing in school. I have some teachers who know about my problems and they would give me extra time to do assignments, but some of my teachers are so strict that I'm too scared to ask for extensions. 

But it's not just problems at home that I have, but I also have some problems at school. Throughout 3 years in a row, I had to go from multiple friend groups because I either felt left out or I was kicked out. There was one time last year where I was in this friend group who I played volleyball with. They were all fine with me one day but then suddenly started ignoring me another day, so I just left and then found out a few weeks later from another friend that those people in that friend group never liked me. There was another friend group that I just left recently because they all would ignore me, sometimes they would talk to me, but I would usually be ignored most of the time. 

I've just been feeling like giving up recently, even though I don't want to at the same time.

Idk i have been just feeling, sad? idk how to describe it. It never occurred to me that i had an underbite until the 6th grade. It also never bothered me who talked about it, I just brushed it off like nothing ever happened. No matter how many times i’ve been told to ignored them, i just can’t? First it started of with a guy in my class who talked about it and asked me to do the same “joker face”. We were playing spin the bottle and it landed on me, then that happened. i refused, knowing what he meant immediately. I always tried ignoring it. but other people caught on, they started talking about it. I’m upset over it, but not until someone i liked, actually dated for 7 months said it. In his words, “I honestly think you should get braces and i like you with your glasses on more”. That was my last straw, even though i still liked him, i miss talking to him. It’s nice to have someone to talk to, to play games with, to just yap about stuff to after school. P.S we are not from the same school, we knew each other by a friend and online dated. Same age, same birthday, interests, childhood too. I could go on about him. We also met each other outside a few times, mostly awkward interactions. But he broke up with me and said that. which hurt me very much. Why? Why would i take someone’s words so seriously when i said i wouldn’t? Well, i liked him, i loved him, from your perspective it may seem like everything is too quick but it’s true. That hurt me so much, until the point i started to think of begging my sister to get me braces, I can’t really stand the hate, the jokes, whatver they are saying. It hurts. It really does. A lot. I cope through all this with a lot of things, nearly developing ED, SH, crying, sleeping, walks but all i can think of is running into a car. When i try expressing my feelings about this towards my family, they all say, suck it up, and not care about what those people have to say. Braces, financially, there’s a thing here where you pay $50/month for it. I told them, you pay for it and if my business goes well in that month, i’ll pay the remaining. No. No one took me on that offer. Everyday, all i can ask myself, “Am i really that ugly? That ugly, til the point no one would make exceptions for you?” I’m just an ordinary girl who is trying to live her life, what more do you ever want from me? I can never look myself in the mirror the same way ever again. I’m hurt, really hurt. But who knows when i’ll ever take it to the next level and eventually.. end it all. Being bullied since 4th grade until my last grade, 10th. really? can life get any worse? and me getting a boyfriend and he left me was worse. I really thought i have someone who’d love me, be there for me when things get rough. But all i can do is not open up to him at all. And being ugly with an underbite on top of that makes it a cherry on top.

A good friend of mine whos a few years younger than me (we'll call him Monty) is your classic sunshine friend, he's funny, smart, kind, overall just sunshine. He's a bit younger than the rest of us, but we love him like a little brother. Lately he's starting to spiral in mental health and we all have tried to help him,  but it just seems like no matter how hard we try, we can't stop/help him.

My other friend (Teio) and I are kind of the older mental health veterans of the friend group, having struggled with eating problems, body image issues, self-harm, etc, and we know the struggle well. So we have been doing everything we can to keep Monty from going down the same road we went down; staying up all night letting him vent, making sure he gets food every day, giving advice when he needs it, and overall just keeping an eye on him. 

But he still fell down the damn hole and I feel like shit. Monty told us that he'd self-harmed once previously, but wouldn't do it again. Teiyo's devasted and blames himself, I talked to him and told him it's not his fault, but he's still not taking it well at all. 

Monty came over today wearing a jacket midsummer and acting like nothing was wrong when we both fucking knew what was happening but neither of us could bring ourselves to say anything. He took off his coat at my place and I saw the damage. Luckily, it didn't look bad enough to need medical attention, but it was still bad and just a lot worse than I had anticipated. 

Im the oldest of the group. I've always been the 'dad friend'. The leader. The one making sure everyone was ok and happy, and now I can't even do that. Everything's falling to shit and I feel like I could have done more to prevent this from happening, even though I know that Monty made his own decisions and I can't force him to do/not do anything. I couldn't stop Monty from self-harming, I can't make Teiyo feel any better about it, I can't keep our other friend from growing more and more distant, but I'm so damn disconnected from everything I can't feel much of what I should be feeling, which is only adding to the guilt.

I was made homeless just over a year ago, only a couple weeks before my 16th birthday. Before that, me and my mum were living with an abuser where if I was too loud or could be heard from downstairs then he would yell and blame my mum. Basically, I haven't had a very good home life and my birthdays have been rubbish because of it. I'm still living in a refuge so I can't invite people over. So this year, the plan was that on my actual birthday, I'd go out with my girlfriend and sleep round hers. The next day, wed go to London and then I'd go to my dads, see him, open my presents etc. Then the following day me and my friends would have a party and sleepover at my girlfriends. My actual birthday was lovely and then things started to go a bit wrong. 
My gf was too ill to go to London but started feeling better later but by that point, it was too late to leave. My dad picked me up and he told me that he hadn't got me a card because I "always leave them". Bit weird but I don't care. It's just a card. I thought this assuming I would get some sort of gift. I didn't. My dad didn't get me a single thing. The only thing I asked him for was a new phone because mine is super broken. That's not an outrageous ask considering my mum got me a £400 laptop and we're homeless while he owns 2 houses. But no phone to be seen. He tells me that I never told him the phone I wanted. I did. I actually pointed out the exact one on his laptop that I wanted but he just forgot to get it. He did do some things though. He put £30 in my account. £30 isn't enough for 2 weeks of busses to and from college. He spent £30 on me while my homeless mother spent almost £500. What is the excuse? Tell you what he did get me. Just before he picked me up, he went to the shops to buy some beer for himself and a few brownie bites. I get home and after an hour go to eat them and half of them are gone. Are you kidding me.
So I'm a bit pissed off but I'm not gonna cry about it. Like this is a new low for him but I wasn't expecting much anyway. Then my girlfriends dad (Rob) decides that my girlfriend is too ill to have people over. For the record, she lost her voice and insisted she was fine. I saw her when she was ill. She was fine. So I start bawling. I was so so excited to have a proper sleepover that felt like it was mine. It's standard for other people but such an honour for me. After my horrible 16th birthday of absolute misery, I needed this one to be good. I needed a break but for it to be snatched away less than 24 hours before it was supposed to happen was such a cruel shock and left me devastated. I was supposed to be going to London and that didn't happen. I was supposed to get presents from my dad and that didn't happen. I was supposed to be having my first happy birthday party in years and now that wasn't going to happen. My gf spent 40 minutes begging her dad to go back on it and let the party happen and eventually he agreed to it. The entire time I was wailing. My eyes were puffy and it had felt like a sword in the heart but atleast the party was on.
For hours the party was going really well. Everyone was having fun and drinking and until about 1 am it was all okay. And then one of my friends (L) bit the other (M). L asked if that made them uncomfy. M said yes and asked for them to never do it again and L apologised. M showed me the mark but my girlfriends parents saw and got very angry that he was bit. Next thing I know, L is in the kitchen sobbing on the floor with my other friends trying to get them to calm down. They felt really guilty and genuinely started having a meltdown. I go over and try and comfort them and say "you apologised. We move on. You're a lovely person, you're just hammered." But then Rob, my girlfriends dad comes in. He starts berating L. Saying that it's unacceptable and wrong. Which okay yes but they know this. This isn't productive. Then he starts mentioning their self harm and their mental health issues and saying that they're unwell and they can't act in this way. By this point I have to tell Rob to stop. He's usually a lovely guy but I know how severe L's mental health is and being berated by a man you met only a few hours earlier while you're sobbing on the floor sounds just a little bit traumatic if you ask me. So me, a very drunk 17 year old, is having to ask this middle aged man to leave my friend alone. He kept defending himself saying that he's "just trying to understand what happened" but anytime L tried to speak he would say "you're in my house now. You have to listen to me." Like bitch, this young, fragile girl is sobbing at your feet because of how guilty they feel and you continue to explain why they should feel ashamed of themselves. It was a bite. No blood was drawn. L was just incredibly drunk and tried to play fight but played too hard.
Then, L and Rob went outside to talk and I ended up talking alone with Di, my gfs mum. I am very good at arguing because I don't get emotional or raise my voice. It is one undeniable thing about myself that I am great with confrontation and I don't get heated. Di, however is famous for the opposite. I won't get into it but her getting angry is not something people want to see. However, as her daughter-in-law, I doubted she would get too mad at me and if she did, it wouldn't bother me. So I tried to make her see where I was coming from that this had been blown out of proportion but she just kept saying she's worried about M. I said that if her priority is M then you should listen to him when he says it's water under a bridge. That this should have ended ages ago and not turned into a huge thing. Apparently, L had been making M uncomfy a lot by asking for hugs all night. My apologies but that is not a big deal. And maybe he really was still upset about being bit but you can't do anything unless he tells you that. Otherwise you're just assuming and getting involved with something that had nothing to do with you. So Di is getting noticably angrier but frankly I don't care. Di will live and get over this but L is different. I care more about L's feelings because their feelings are bigger. So my gf walks in because she doesn't want me to be alone with her mum while she's angry but then Di started taking it out on my gf and that sent me into tears. 1. because i felt slightly responsible and 2. I don't care if you're her mum. You're angry at me, you don't take it out on the love of my life.
So me and my gf went upstairs and I am sobbing. There's no salvaging the sleepover. It's ruined. Other people started crying as well. People are considering going home. But this was my special day. Not just any old birthday party, but a slice of normality I havent had for so long. This was so so important to me and I was so distraught. Rob came up to talk to me. He said "I love you to bits but this is my house and if you have a problem with Di then you have a problem with me. Not Di in this house, okay?" I understand. It's his wife. But I was never going to do anything different. He eventually leaves and I really just can't stop crying. Im in a ball on my gfs bed just going "Im so sorry" to her over and over again because I'm so sad I've made her parents angry and upset her. She said I should have just not said anything and I explained that I would have been a bad friend if I had just sat there and let Di and Rob say whatever they wanted. My gf understood. I put on doctor who and went to sleep. 

I'm just so upset my birthday caused so much upset for people. It was supposed to be fun for everyone but everyone was so miserable. :((((((

Quiero contar algo que me tiene un poco pensativo. Yo estudio en una universidad y voy en el segundo ciclo, en el primer ciclo que empezó en marzo de este año conocí a una chica que llamó mi atención, me pareció muy bonita e interesante a primera vista. Como me interesé en ella quise invitarla a salir, como no quería que sea muy lanzado ( o sea, quería ir despacio). Entonces traté de acercarme a ella por medio de sus amigos, me hice a migo de uno y logré entrar en un grupo de trabajo , de la universidad, con ella. Entonces mi excusa para  hablarle era que éramos compañeros de equipo, por lo tanto conocernos sería adecuado. La cuestión es que le hablé una vez que coincidimos al ingresar a la universidad, solo le pregunté sobre sus cursos y cómo se sentía. Con esa pequeña conversación no sentí más interés que antes pero como fue una buena conversación decidí invitarla a salir. Pero como estábamos en el mismo grupo de trabajo pensé en que sería incómodo si es que no salían bien las cosas. Menos mal que no la invité porque el primer día que nos reunimos para conversar sobre el proyecto ella llamó a su novio por teléfono a preguntarle si nos podía ayudar con el diseño del producto audiovisual, de eso era el proyecto presentar un video o parecido. No oí lo que conversaron porque me concentré en que tenía novio ,no sé si aún es sus novio supongo que sí. Fue decepcionante para mí ,pero ella tampoco me había dado señales de que le gustara o eso creo ( solo eran miradas fijas hacia mí ,supongo que las malinterpreté pensando que al menos ella tenía curiosidad sobre mí). Luego de haber escuchado esa parte de la interacción con su novio solo pensé en que ella lo hacía de manera deliberada, o sea para que sepa que no debía intentar coquetearle, porque la llamada la hizo cuando estábamos reunidos en grupo y en voz alta. Eso no me afectó mucho puesto que es muy atractivo que una mujer de a entender que no quiere nada con nadie excepto con su novio. En las reuniones de grupo podía escuchar sus ideas no solo para el trabajo sino en general, porque a veces nos desviábamos del tema por aburrimiento , lo normal. Y en serio que sus ideas eran muy atractivas, su forma de pensar era muy interesante , además de que sabía poner límites. Si no le parecía inapropiado hablar sobre temas como sexualidad lo decía y lo hacía de forma respetuosa, sus reacciones también eran lindas. Pero, como mencioné ella tenía novio y lo dejó en claro con esa llamada en la reunión de grupo. Por eso decidí no intervenir , ya que mi moral no me permite hacerlo. No me metería en la relación de ellos nunca, porque debe sentirse horrible y es irrespetuoso. El proyecto se presentó y fue un buen trabajo. Luego, en agosto que empezó el segundo ciclo, yo decidí enviar un mensaje en el grupo que se había creado para realizar el proyecto y que nadie había eliminado. Con ese mensaje mi intensión era saber sobre la chica, tal vez y ya había terminado con el novio. Fue un mensaje también para hablar como amigos con todos los miembros de ese grupo, contestaron a mi mensaje y la chica fue la primera. Claro que ese último dato no dice nada en realidad, pero me pareció interesante. Les pregunté sobre sus horarios ya que no había visto a ninguno en la universidad. Ella contestó  que se encontraba en la universidad almorzando , eran las 12:00 pm. En ese momento yo también me encontraba almorzando, entonces le pregunté directamente, en el mismo grupo pero respondiendo a su mensaje, dónde se encontraba para poder almorzar juntos. Ella no respondió, ni siquiera vio el mensaje. En ese momento, nadie envió mensaje, el grupo de quedó con mi invitación hacia ella como último mensaje por 1 hora. Luego, una amiga me respondió pero se sintió el momento incomodo por la falta de respuesta de ella. Obviamente me afectó porque me ignoró, ese día lo tomé relajado como una pequeñes que me había dado a entender que era mejor no hacerle ese tipo de proposiciones. Sin embargo, luego de unos días no podía sacar ese tema de la cabeza entonces lo pensé mejor y llegué a la conclusión de que no le intereso y ni siquiera como amigo , a parte de que es una falta de consideración no responder a la invitación. Aunque sea un "no" es aceptable, pero que me haya ignorado deja en claro que no debí hacer eso y que no tiene consideración conmigo. Dos semanas después la vi en un edificio de la universidad , dentro del campus. Yo me dirigía a ese edificio porque tiene espacio para descansar (recostarte o echarte). Subí al tercer piso ,donde se encuentra ese espacio, allí la vi. Ese piso tiene dos pasadizos separados por un muro. Ella se encontraba conversando con dos personas que no vi bien, me concentré en ella, cerca de las escaleras. Pero como dije ese lugar tiene dos pasadizos, caminé por el otro pasadizo porque me puse nervioso, algo raro en mí, no sé si me vio pero me fui a descansar. Después de haber descansado me dirigía al ascensor, para poder salir del edificio e ir a mis clases. Volví a verla en el mismo pasadizo pero esta vez si me vio. Me puse nervioso de nuevo pero no podía usar el otro pasadizo porque al ser estrecho no cabe mucha gente y habían personas en ese lado. Caminé por el costado de ella rápido porque en serio estaba nervioso y no quería saludar porque recordé lo del mensaje y que me ignoró. Ahora sé que no estuvo bien, debí saludar aunque sea como muestra de respeto pero bueno... Yo decidí no hablarle porque considero que es lo mejor, cuando me interesa alguien creo que se me nota y no estaría cómodo siendo su amigo pero queriendo otra cosa. Prefiero no tentarme, si estoy cerca de ella creo que le coquetearía y no está bien por el respeto que se debe tener a todas las relaciones amorosas. Además, las dos ocasiones que considero que fueron para decirme que no intentara nada, como poniendo límites. Y lo malo es que me gusta que ponga límites, es atractivo. Este tema es el que me incomoda y supongo que me ayudará si al menos lo escribo, a nadie le conté sobre el mensaje. Para mí fue muy especial que se me haga atractiva e interesante ella, fue como que la noté. No me había pasado por tres años, claramente los mismos que estoy soltero. Gracias.

Even though my cousin is not doing that well in school her parents still comfort her, and she has an amazing older sister who gets her feelings. I don't dislike her though, I dislike the fact that I have no one to comfort me. And the reason why I do well in school is because of the people who give me motivation to go to school every single day and it's not my parents, nor my siblings it's them, my friends. I'm in the highest section in school, I may not be the smartest but I know that I am just smarter than some people, but that's not what I want. I would trade every single one of my academic certificates, every single one of my medals just for someone who can comfort me. But I know it's not a strong enough reason to end my life, because that's stupid but all I'll ever ask for is for someone to actually understand me. I'm not looking for someone who takes my side whenever I'm in a fight with someone, I'm looking for someone who gets me, someone who'll tell me that it's fine and the best thing to do is process your feelings instead of trying to push them away. Today on December 30, a day before new year I tried waking up a little early because my mother told us to clean. I woke up at 9am and ate breakfast, I washed my bag after that I took a broom and started sweeping our floors, I asked my older brother where the mop was and started mopping, my sister woke up at 11am and I was mopping at that time, she looked over at me and said "Stop mopping just be on your phone because you're not doing anything" she told me, I ignored her since I've broomed over our floors two times before I started mopping, she kept bothering me about it and telling me that it was useless, I should just be on my phone and that she'll clean again later, she said it in such a mean way that I stormed to my room and started crying, it was such a small issue so I tried to stop myself from crying, but I just couldn't. A hour passed by, and there I was crying inside my bathroom. I had been crying so much that I had difficulty breathing, I heard my mother coming back from shopping and I tried to compose myself, but I had been crying my eyes our for so long that they were sore. She saw me and said that we'll talk, I told her that I tried helping my sister with cleaning but she disliked the way I did it, I apologized to my sister with a shaky voice, she just scoffed at me. My mother told me gobble up my feelings because crying wasn't gonna fix things, I told her that I've tried numerous times trying to stop myself but I couldn't, she then told me to be on my phone so I'll be less sad... I'm just tired of being the only one in my friend groups who's mentally stable enough to listen to peoples problems and hardships and general complaints, but no one has the bandwidth to do so for me.

I can't just... complain about the B.S. life is throwing my way, and my friends' way, that's stressing me out otherwise it will further stress them out. And I know if they get any more stressed, they could snap.

No one else in my groups in equipped to Actually handle being the shoulder to lean on right now. Life is hitting everyone hard, and I have enough awareness of others during conversations that I know when someone is struggling to listen because of the heavy topic affecting their mental health but will persevere because they love me.

I don't want to put that strain on anyone who can't shoulder the burden without breaking. And it sucks, but none of my friends are in a place right now, and haven't been in a place for a while, to do so.

I'm the one constant in everyone's lives. Even when I'm not doing my best, I'm still good, because my strength of resolve was forged in horrific abuse as a child that I just... sheer force of willed my way through. I have a very very high tolerance of stress before it starts affecting me in any significant way.

The trade off for that is once it does get to the tipping point, it goes from 'Everything's fine and manageable' to 'catastrophic failure'. I'm self aware enough to know this about myself, and know I'm approaching that tipping point alarmingly fast right now.

I'm struggling through SADs, post-election nonsense, post convention depression where I had almost all my loved ones in very close proximity for an entire long weekend and we had so much fun and now they're all back scattered across the country and I'm at my own home alone and tired and in pain, and my usual chronic illness pain is being outshined by my torn back muscle that connects to my spine.

That's just the surface stuff right now, that I really just want to complain about for like an hour to someone I know and love and get it off my chest and out of my head so I can focus and re-calibrate for doing actual productive things.

But I can't, because no one else can be that shoulder I can cry on even if they really do mean it when they say I can come to them any time. I know they mean it, and I know they want to be there for me when I'm hurting, but I also know they aren't in a place to actually fulfill that task.

I'm tired, I'm frustrated, I'm in a crazy amount of pain that I can't just willfully ignore right now, I'm missing all my friends and family, I'm dreading talking to my low contact mother, I'm having spousal issues of them just... not being mindful about stuff and giving empty promises, and dreading having a conversation about 'hey, just stop promising to do stuff and things will actually be better for it, than giving me a promise that gets broken, that we both know will be broken, that's only going to make me upset'

I'm just so tired.

I don't wanna like, be a jerk and throw a temper tantrum or anything like that, but it'd be nice if I could be the one being given grace for once than the one always having to give the grace and benefit of the doubt.

I understand now and have a better picture, but I must be more careful. It didn't matter who I reached out to when it happened. I keep meeting people who have awful behavior no matter where I go online, and that concerns me.

I mean, people didn't seem concerned because I got paid, but it didn't matter that my safety and my life mattered more than being paid, and her behavior was affected by my mental health and my trust in authority. Even though not everyone was the same, I still felt hurt. It's not my only odd encounter, but I feel awful because it upset me and made me glad I kept to myself and listened to myself more.

I'm glad I trust my instincts a lot. They revealed their true nature later, but I already knew how they were.

I'm glad I knew what to do to protect myself. I am also proud of myself, which shows that I know how to read others well. I didn't think I could, considering I have autism and Adhd, but I'm glad, and I am surprised by that skill. I am curious to know how many people have it.

But I'm still shocked by it. Also, many people have called me stupid and still do. I am still determining where this comes from, but I want to discuss it. What they say isn't true, but I don't know why people keep repeating that response. I never did anything to warrant that.

I never experienced this in a grocery store when I was shopping, but it happens often in the mountains where people want to bully me, and I'm wondering what's happening. I know I'm developmentally delayed because of autism, and I buy what I like, and I'm happy about it.

First, my 93-year-old great-grandmom went to the hospital in 2022 for mild COVID symptoms. While she was there, doctors found a small, non-cancerous mass on her kidney. She needed a nephrostomy tube and had limited mobility. She used to live with my uncle, but he had a stroke and couldn't take care of her anymore. Instead of sending her to a nursing home, my mom brought her to live with us. We live in a two-bedroom townhouse, so my mom put a temporary bed for her in the living room. 

She's nice, but her singing and humming at night really annoy me. I remind her sometimes, and she'll stop for a few nights, but when I tell my mom, she either dismisses me or says to just turn my TV up to full volume and never does anything about it. I've lost sleep because of it. My mom hasn't been able to do much either since she lost her job almost a year ago and has been looking for work. 

Fast forward to April 2024, my great-grandmom is now very needy and calls my mom downstairs throughout the day. She also has annoying habits like picking her teeth and constantly asking what I'm doing or where I'm going. I miss when it was just my mom and me at home. I never wanted my great-grandmom to live with us. Looking at my friends, they're so lucky they don't have elderly people in their homes.

Well before I say all this I need to point out that I’m only 16. I just don’t know who to tell. And so if you read something and think that’s it’s not a big deal just keep in mind that to me it is. Okay this is everything In order. I became friends with this guy at school a few months ago. Really nice guy. We’re calling him James. Well a week after we because friends My parents told me they’re separating and I knew it was gonna happen but it still sucks. So that night I needed to talk to someone so I called James and then walked an hour to his side of the city and we talked for a while before he had to go home. It helped. I never usually talk to people about my issues. That weekend I got drunk for the first time and I had about 20 shots of vodka in 2 hours and so I called James and my bestfriend and told them all my depressing secrets so now they knew EVERYTHING. Let’s call my bsf lily. Then school holidays came around. I was feeling pretty happy tbh. Not depressed. James came over for the first time that week and we had a great time. He came over 3 more times within the week. Until midnight everytime. The 4th time he kissed me and that was my first kiss. And obviously I had a fat crush on him since the first time we hung out at my house. Before he kissed me he told me he didn’t want a relationship and he didn’t want me to tell anyone. I didn’t think I could handle a relationship anyways right now so I said I didn’t mind. 2 days later we both went to lilys party and he kissed me again but he told Lilly that he doesn’t have feelings for me. I get drunk again the night after the party and tell James I want to die. And then 2 days later again he tried to kiss me but I didn’t kiss him because I didn’t want to kiss someone that didn’t even have some feelings for me. Then I became friends with James’ friend Nate. Nate and I are still friends. A few days later I cut myself again. For the first time in weeks. And I call James because k was scared. I trust him so so much. Not even a few days later though James tells me he’s dating someone. Obviously I’m upset but I tell him I’m happy for him. That night I was talking to Lilly about it all and she told me that he’s been dating that girl for weeks. And that I cheated with him without knowing and she couldn’t tell me because she wanted James to tell me himself. Then me and Nate get talking and he explains it all to me. To keep it short il just say that he’s been with this girl the whole time and that he cheated on her with me the first day they dated. I felt terrible: so I got drunk again. My 4th time being drunk. Within the month. I hadn’t drunk any before September. So anyways I’ve been so sad lately. Then there was a party last weekend. Everyone was there. I got so drunk th at Nate almost called the ambulance because I was paralysed. My eyes are still yellow and bloody. Everyone is talking about it though. Saying how embarrassing it is for me and that I was fake drunk to get attention. They have said so much shit about me. And so now I’ve lost friends. James hasn’t really spoken to me in weeks anyways but now he just doesn’t even answer if I do talk to him. And he just always looks mad at me. The night after the party I almost killed myself because of everything building up. My anxiety and depression, anorexia, family and friends. I just didn’t want to be here anymore. And today I felt a bit better but no one had been talking to me other than Lilly and a few friends. I just don’t know what to do. I feel horrible about getting so drunk. And I know I have a lot of shit going on and I obviously won’t be the happiest person to be around. I just hate being sad because everyone leaves after I’m sad all the time. I just want my friends back.

I hate the way they cry when my father hits them, I hate how they’re just animals doing things by instinct yet they have to deal with my father’s slaps and kicks, even more to be honest. My mother since always and said herself that she’s a clean freak, a single clothing on the floor and the room is imperfect for her: so she has gotten herself to place her addiction on me, at first as a toddler, it wasn’t that bad but annoying, then annoying slowly through the years I started to see cleaning as a punishment by how my mother treated it, I rely on internet a lot to escape from my life, and every single time she’s not satisfied with my room, she takes my only way of happiness away, the internet despite me having quite unlimited access to it, I was almost groomed once too. 
Getting again on the theme that my mother punishes me by cleaning yes, I feel like a disappointment, my brother is even way more cleaner to me and he’s a boy and I’m a “girl”, am I not supposed to be naturally clean? Then why is that my brother is better than me in what i am supposed to be by nature? I don’t know but the only way I can get over the feeling of guilt is by being a  great student at school, no fighting, no bad grades, always pleasing my teachers and classmates, you know it.. and doing that is exhausting, I take all my energy on being an excellent student, im killing myself by doing the most I can. Also im autistic and got diagnosed last year but apparently i don’t show that many traits due masking, but still im autistic: loud sounds overwhelms me and being in a room with those sounds for 5 hours makes me feel worst at school, I genuinely feel that I can’t be myself, I feel that the only way I can fit in is by being smart and responsible: but never by being myself. It’s exhausting really, and I can’t be tidy exhausted and fatigated, I can’t enjoy my hyperfixations exhausted, I can’t enjoy myself fatigated, I can’t live tired of life. I’m 11, soon 12 the next year. I feel like dramatic because there are people suffering more than me. I sometimes think that being messy is the only way I can express my agony but it will always be portrayed as “a result of laziness.”

I want help, I want to be myself. I don’t know if I explained myself well but if you read this all without judging then thanks.

For most of my life, I was told I was attractive. Friends, family, and even strangers would comment on my looks, often with a sense of admiration or envy. I should have felt confident, self-assured, and ready to embrace life with open arms. Yet, behind the compliments and outward appearances, there was a part of me that felt incomplete, ashamed, and unworthy. This feeling stemmed from a personal tragedy that shattered my self-esteem, distorted my self-image, and left deep scars on my mental health: the loss of a testicle. The incident happened when I was 14, and it felt like my world collapsed in an instant. One day, I was a typical, carefree teenager with dreams, desires, and a sense of invincibility. The next, I was grappling with a loss that I couldn’t fully understand or accept. The physical pain was one thing, but the emotional and psychological turmoil was something I hadn’t been prepared for. Losing a part of myself that was so deeply tied to my identity as a man left me feeling like I had lost much more than just a testicle. In the years that followed, I struggled silently with the impact of that loss. While people still told me I was attractive, I could never fully believe them. How could I, when I felt so fundamentally flawed? My self-esteem took a nosedive. I began to see myself not as the person others saw, but as someone broken, damaged, and less than a man. I questioned my worth, not just in terms of physical appearance, but as a person deserving of love and intimacy. Despite the compliments and attention, I have never had a sexual relationship, or any long-term relationship for that matter. The fear of rejection, of someone discovering my “imperfection,” was too overwhelming. I couldn’t bear the thought of exposing myself, both physically and emotionally, only to be judged or pitied. The loss of a testicle became a barrier between me and the intimacy I craved. The shame and insecurity created a vicious cycle; the more I avoided relationships, the more isolated and unworthy I felt. This struggle affected my self-image in profound ways. I no longer saw myself as whole or desirable. Every time I looked in the mirror, I saw the absence, the void that reminded me of what I had lost. It became a constant source of anxiety and self-doubt. In social situations, I would compare myself to other men, feeling like I was somehow lesser, even though no one else knew my secret. The physical change translated into a psychological burden that weighed on me daily. 

I'm 18 now, and I’m standing at a crossroads. After years of insecurity, a possible solution has opened—a replacement surgery. This is an opportunity for me to regain a sense of normalcy and to finally feel normal. But as I face this decision, I’m torn. The surgery is scheduled for the first week of university, freshers. To complicate matters further, I’ve just recently started climbing, a sport that I've quickly become obsessed with.  
On one hand, the replacement surgery feels like a chance to close a painful chapter in my life. The thought of having a testicular implant offers hope—a way to restore what was lost, at least in a physical sense. It could be the solution that helps me move past the insecurities that have plagued me for so long. The idea of looking in the mirror and seeing a body that feels whole again is incredibly appealing. It’s a chance to step into university life with a newfound sense of confidence, to meet new people and form relationships without the fear of rejection that has held me back for years. But the timing couldn’t be worse. University is a huge milestone, a moment I’ve been looking forward to for as long as I can remember. The first week is critical—an opportunity to make new friends, settle into a new environment, and start building the foundation for the next chapter of my life. Missing out on that because of surgery feels like I’d be trading one set of challenges for another. Would I be sacrificing the chance to make a fresh start socially and academically, only to find myself struggling again with feelings of isolation and inadequacy? Any advice?

I had two friends, one a guy and the other a girl. We were all online friends for years, me and the guy were friends since I was in elementary school.

Starting with the guy who from here on I’ll call Blake (not his real name of course) me and Blake were the best of buds, we played the game basically every day, and regardless of our different lives and us only being online friends, I actually liked Blake a lot (as a friend). But Blake had a tendency of bullying me, which didn't bother me too much when I was younger, but as I got older, I did. For example, I told Blake one time that I failed/got held back in first grade, and he would bring that fact up whenever anything intelligence-wise come up or just bring it up out the blue. “Nigga you failed the first grade” or “ok…flunky” and other variations. He would say all that…even though first grade was a million years ago (I’m a senior about to graduate high school, and I haven’t gotten held back or struggled with grades since.). Like I'm about to graduate and he would still make fun of me with that fact, saying stuff like "you would've graduated soon if you didn't fail the first grade" and if I tried to rebuttal saying how I do well in school, he would say "well you did the first grade twice so of course you'd do better. you've done it twice".

He would constantly compare his life to mine, making fun of how I’m a virgin who never done anything with a girl, my social anxiety, me being a loner, how I don’t have as much money as him, how my parents are not as laid-back as his, how I didn’t have as much money as him, make fun about me being into 2D girls, my music taste, and more.

He was controlling while we played games. He would always decide what games we played, and he would bitch and moan when I would get onto a game he wanted. He would always be on his phone or just be otherwise distracted by long periods of time when we're playing, and then he would expect me to wait for him. He would be very controlling when we play a game like R6s, he would be in my ear after he died ordering me around and telling me what to do and where to go, expecting me to get kills when I’m completely trash at the game, then he would complain heavily when I would of course, die.

There is way more things he would do but if I typed them all out, I would be typing all damn day, so I’ll stop here.

Next is the girl, who I’ll refer to as Amber.
Amber was not so bad, she was annoying at times, and she would sometimes mimic the things Blake did but overall, she was alright.
It got to the point where I would be so fed up with them, mostly with Blake, that I would ghost them, not talk or play with them for a few days to basically recover before interacting with the two again. I would even groan in annoyance when either one of them would call me.

Now I know what you guys are thinking, "why didn't you tell them how you felt about their treatment of you???", well, I tried, but I would just be met with being called a "pussy" or "soft" and the behavior wouldn't change.

Now let’s go back about three weeks ago, April 1st, my 19th birthday, Amber called me up and told me to hop on the game, I was in the ignoring them phase, but I decided to hop on because they wouldn’t be mean to me, right? Wrong, they told me to install rocket league, a game I’ve never played in my entire life and when I was able to join their game and we got into a match, I was obviously performing very very poorly, but that didn’t stop them very berating me and telling me how trash I am…even though they been playing longer than me.
Then we got on Roblox, and I was having technical difficulties because I was using the Microsoft store version of Roblox as I didn’t know that there was an actual Roblox client and launcher. when the two found out that I was using the Microsoft store version, they proceeded to berate me and belittle me some more, calling me stupid and dumb and questioning how in the world did I not know about the Roblox launcher and just basically making it feel like I just committed a cardinal sin. Then we play some Roblox, and somehow someway I kept on doing things wrong and games were playing because I kept on getting berated and belittled. Eventually, I got off.
I didn’t talk to them on the second, but I did talk to them and play with them on the third. We ended up playing Roblox again and just like last time I was getting berated and belittled because I would do things that they didn’t like. And after a couple hours, we eventually stopped playing Roblox and it suddenly goes into Blake diss tracking me. He would find random rap instrumentals on YouTube and then he would rap over them dissing me. He then told me to diss him back but I didn’t want to cause I was already in a bad mood, and I’m not good with words so I would make a fool of myself. And despite me saying multiple times that I didn’t want to rap, they both kept saying that I had to rap, eventually I just unplugged my mic and said that my mic was broken, which caused Blake to be like “if you don’t want to rap just say that dude, you don’t have to do all that”, like, bruh, I said I didn’t want to rap multiple times…

Eventually, I left a discord call and went to sleep because I had school in the morning, and when I woke up in the morning and checked my Discord, I saw that I had a message from Blake and the message said “pussy”. After that, I ignored them again until the 6th. When I took my phone off chill mode (do not disturb) I noticed that Blake unfollowed me on Instagram and that he kicked me out of the discord server. I was like "damn", but I just went on about my day, Amber was still following me though.

Now today, the 25th, I realized that Amber unfollowed me on Instagram as well, and that they both blocked me on Steam. So, I thought our friendship was over, so decide to unfollow both of them on Instagram, Discord, Steam, and delete both of their contacts from my phone.

I wasn't perfect in the friendship either, but I never berate or belittle either of them, bully and make fun of either of them constantly, make either of them feel inferior. never...

part of me feel relief that I no longer have to deal with them anymore, but another part of me feels sad as they were both my only friends. I don't really know how to feel or if I should try to reach out to them somehow and apologize for ghosting them.

I don't know man...I just really want to get this off my chest...
