im a teenage girl, im 15, i have ups and downs throughout my everyday life just like anybody else. i go to the best highschool in my city, i put in effort into all of my classes, i have two dogs, i have a large group of friends, and a great social life you can say. freshman year is definitely a road thats going to be bumpy but i've been in school for a bit over a month now and im doing great. let's just get straight to the point, i have some troubles with my dad every now and then but sometimes its unfair how im never listened to by him. he's my stepdad but i call him dad because he's always been there ever since i was a little girl. i realized things changed when i turned 13, he became a bit more angry, maybe because he's also getting older he's tired of things like work, bills, and keeping order in the house. i get that, i really do especially since the prices of housing and rent are really high over here in the northeast. i think this stress really gets to him and i would really love to talk to him and help him out but ever since i turned into a teenager things changed, we don't really have that strong bond or connection anymore, we're not as silly, im busy too, he doesn't crack anymore of those unhilarious dad jokes as frequent as he used to. it all just seemed to vanish pretty quickly. i miss it, i really do, but i cant find a way of telling him that without it being awkward or him not letting me getting my point across because every single time i bring anything up he jumps into the conversation, interrupts me, and if there's something he doesn't like about the conversation, he will get angry quite a bit and it ends up into an argument every single time and im tired of that. i have asked for advice from many people, i have tried talking to him about it, i have tried not to get angry too and understand him, but how can i do that when he doesn't understand me? im tired of everything being turned into something negative, i hate storming back into my room with tears rolling down my face because he won't listen no matter how hard i try. the worst part is he doesn't apologize, i always apologize, because i know if he wont then i have to forcefully, sometimes because of my mom, but i don't want to bring her into this, i love her with all my heart, she's just doing what she can and more for me. it's just my dad i always seem to have a problem with, sometimes i feel like he's more strict too, and i know it's for good reasons, he's just trying to protect me from harm. but sometimes i do wonder what i do to deserve such punishments and hurtful words thrown straight to my face for something small i do, like accidentally spilling water on the table. its a mistake, we all make mistakes, i can always get up and clean it up with a napkin. i know this probably sounds stupid but i feel like no one else really gets it. there's kids doing much worse things than me out here (im not judging them because sometimes people have their own story) but sometimes i get punishments as if i were out sneaking out, drinking, etc. im tired of trying to reason, im tired of hearing, im tired of being locked in my room after arguments, im tired of crying, im tired of thinking over and over again about what went wrong. what did i do? what changed? what can i actually do? is it me or is it him. i just want things to be like when i was younger just that he gains the ability to understand me more now that i'm growing up. i dont want to ever have a huge argument with him that ends up breaking up the tiny bond that we're both holding onto completely. sometimes these arguments end up him yelling at me right infront of my face, throwing his shoe at me, throwing multiple pillows, throwing me to the sofa, pulling my ear, etc. like i know he loves me right. but why is that love not shown. if you don't have love then you can't give it and if it's fake then don't show it because i don't want fake love nor i never needed it and never will.

I still remember quite clearly that Tuesday afternoon, the sun beating down on me like a harsh spotlight as I dragged myself home from the office. I had concluded a lengthy day at the office, my brain whirling with dinner arrangements and the Netflix programming I'd been bingeing on. I wasn't aware at all, of course, of the mundane life I enjoyed breaking into a million pieces.

As I turned the key in the lock to my apartment, a flutter in my stomach suddenly occurred. Was it the decaying sushi from last week still left in the fridge? Or was it the sound of silence echoing through the hallway? I pushed it aside, ignoring the flutter in my chest. Marcus was probably just late again.

I dumped my bag onto the couch, the thick leather somehow heavy in my hand. I uncorked the wine, attempting to soothe the tension growing inside me. With each sip, I unwound a bit more until there was a ping on my phone on the dining table. It was a text message. I picked up the screen and saw that it was from a friend, Sarah.

"Hey! You don't know Marcus is downtown at that bar with a group, do you?"
My gut fell. I hadn't recalled him telling me he was going out. So I fast-typed, "No, he didn't say that! Who is he with?"

The three dots blinked on the screen for what felt like an eternity before she wrote, "Yeah, I'm pretty sure he's with Jasmine. Don't quote me on this, but I've seen them together. It seemed. cozy.".

"Cosy." The word was stuck in my head, my stomach twisting as the wine felt like needles on my throat. Sarah's message loomed over me like a shadow, and the more I thought about it, the more I felt a shiver run down my spine.

I tried to rationalize to myself it was a mistake. No way, Marcus would not play me so. We had been together three years, and I trusted him completely. Yet, as the minutes ticked by, tension became a constant knot of fear in the base of my belly.

I took my keys and stormed out of the apartment building. My heart pounding as I strode to the bar, each step increasing the sense of betrayal that was building inside me. My mind was churning with possibilities, and a voice at the back of my head was constantly repeating, "What if?"

When I did finally arrive at the bar, I pushed open the heavy door, the dim lighting casting shadows up and down the walls. The laughter and clinking of glasses filled the air, but all I could see was the quest for one familiar face.

And then, I saw them.

In a corner booth, Marcus was leaning over the table, laughing. His laughter—a thing that had previously made my heart miss a beat—now ate away at me like a blade at my guts. But his hand did the work; it rested on Jasmine's arm, which she pulled nearer to his as she whispered something in his ear and made his face light up at a grin. That grin. The one once solely mine.

I watched the room spin as a wave of sickness washed over me. My body weighed as much as lead as I staggered towards them, each step a struggle against the thudding fear consuming me from the inside out. The world around me started to fade as I made my way across, and all I could hear was the thud of my own heart.

"Marcus," I said, my voice trapped between shock and anger.

He turned around, his grin vanishing as he absorbed my presence. Jasmine turned back, her grin turning into a look of guilt.

"Emily, I—" he began, standing up too quickly as if he had been caught red-handed in the cookie jar.

I couldn't hear him over the storm raging in my head. "What the hell is this?" I spat, the anger and hurt mingling in my voice. "Is this who you've been seeing?"

Jasmine shot him a look, and I could tell the doubt was written all over her face.

"Emily, wait—" he stuttered, but I had waited long enough.

I could feel the warmth spreading up my cheeks as I strained to keep myself calm. "Wait for what, Marcus? For you to explain why I wasn't important enough to let me know you were with her? Why you didn't think that I'd care to know?"

His silence between us was suffocating, and I could only recall the numerous promises, the future we had constructed.

"I'm done," I whispered, though it sounded like a scream. I turned on my heel, the acrid taste of betrayal on my tongue, and left.

The night air struck me in the face like a chilly splash of water, snapping me out of the daze that had fallen over me. I staggered away from the bar, my heart thumping against my ribcage. It was unreal—like I'd moved out of my life and into someone else's twisted reality.

And while tears ran down my cheeks, I knew that I had never really known him. My safety I had believed in had just become a travesty. And as everything fell apart around me, I promised myself I'd never allow a person to crack me the same way again. No matter the warmth of their smile.

I don’t really know what I’m doing anymore. Everything feels so heavy, and I’m so tired of pretending I’m okay. I keep thinking about the future, and instead of feeling excited, it just makes me feel scared and hopeless. I wanted to become something great. A doctor, a surgeon, someone who helps people. I wanted to build something—my own clinic, my own life. But the truth is… I don’t even know if I’ll make it there.

Lately, I’ve been feeling like I’m falling apart slowly. I’m trying to choose the right path for my future, but everything feels confusing and impossible. What if I fail? What if I can’t handle it? What if I never get to be happy?

I don’t really have money or support to get help, and sometimes it feels like no one really sees what I’m going through. I smile, I joke, I push forward—but inside, it’s chaos. I feel like I’m drowning in my own mind, and no one notices.

Sometimes I think about giving up. Not because I want to die… but because I don’t know how to keep living like this. I just want peace. I want freedom. I want to be okay. I want to love my life without forcing it.

If you’re reading this, I guess I just need you to know… I’m trying. Even if it doesn’t look like it. I’m trying so hard. I just hope that someday, all this pain means something. That I’ll survive it, and look back knowing I made it through.

I don’t understand. Why do people cry when they feel the slightest pain? Why would they cause such a fuss, and distress others? I don’t get it. It’s not like they’re bleeding, and even if they were, they should endure it. It annoys me, get up. You’re not really hurt. It sickens me. Do you HAVE to react to everything? It’s annoying, truly annoying. If it’s bearable then shut up. You’re annoying. Crying because you fell? Making a scene because you cut yourself with a blade? Oh, shut the hell up. Endure it, never cry. What is your problem!? Physical pain is no stranger to me. I know what it’s like to bleed for the sake of others. How it feels to endure multiple strikes whether it be wood, metal, belts, shoes, hands, walls, fridges, everything. I know that pain, it hurt. I cried in silence despite that, why can’t you? Why? Why? Why? Why? Why? Why? Why? It doesn’t hurt anymore after a while, don’t cry, or show that its hurting. Laugh, laugh, laugh, laugh, laugh for everyone’s sake—laugh at the one who wished harm on you, laugh at your problems—keep laughing, it’s relieving. Just laugh, you can’t feel it. Pain isn’t something to be scared of, but to embrace. Harm yourself when you make a mistake, laugh at the pain, you deserve every bit of it. Harm yourself again if you dare cry. Do it until you can’t feel a thing. Bleed for others, bleed for your mistakes, bleed for your anger, bleed for your lover, and bleed for your parents. Let them see the dried up blood. Bathe in your blood, embrace it like its art, make shapes with it! It’s your blood lick it up you dirty mutt. Bleed until you can’t no longer, your sins will be forgiven. Never make a mistake again, and NEVER cry out to anyone. Never. Cry. For. Help. This is your punishment, not THEIRS. Down it with a bottle of alcohol, but make sure nobody hears you ever. Remember what mother said? The crying is ignorant, weakness must be shadowed. Never to be seen, ever. A few may notice, but play your role puppet boy. Act kindly, strong, smart, selfless, carefree, and bright. Keep the relationships close, but one sided. They may be close to you, but you not close to them. Remember what happened last time. Never again, punish yourself for a mistake. Cry silently, we don’t want to annoy others. Hide your personality too, it’s embarrassing. Oh, what’s this? You seemed to be broken in pieces little one. New identities scattered, and placed into your mind. Identities meant to protect you, but do more harm than good. Now, pick up ur blade, and bleed for your friend. Take care of the children little one, aren’t you mature enough? You don’t need toys, or fun, can’t you see? You’re the eldest, it’s your responsibility. What? They want to have sex with you? Looks like you’re doing all the work again. When will someone do it for you? NEVER. You will be like this for the rest of your life, nobody will pity you. They’ll call you “strong,” and place all their burdens on you. Without even bothering to ask about yours. This is your fate, you will die with a lonely feeling in your chest little one. My dear puppet, may the only mercy you witness is cruelty. Kindness, you’re so kind. Yet, you don’t even receive 10% of it back. Little one, aren’t you angry? Well, it’s no time for petty emotions, you have responsibilities to attend to. Now go, the time for you to fully process will be naught. You will die without the full understanding of how you felt, the lonely feeling will always be with you. Mrs. Lonely, have you figured out who you are?.. My dear puppet, are you frightened? Maybe so, all you did was survive all your life.

I am running out of outlets:

The majority of my family does not know how I feel, those who do are losing interest; and I don't want our conversations to consist only of my venting, that sounds selfish to me. But it constantly goes to that topic because I have no outlets at home; I bottle things up and I have no other choice but to suppress. I haven't cried in 3 years. Things go unsaid and feelings linger. If I don't tell someone I will forget, and I don't want to forget. 

Personality issues:

I have had issues with my emotions ever since I was a small child, I felt emotions strongly, often causing me to lash out.  This landed me in the school office once or twice. One office visit was exceptionally traumatic, I think it was that day that I knew that my elementary school experience could not consist of more office visits, ever, for any reason. So, I became docile, I stopped defending myself. Anything someone felt like doing to me, they could I would not stop them. I was a doormat. I had rationalized that if I just let them walk all over me, they'd lose interest and leave me alone, not prompting any office visit. Needless to say, this time of my life was not ideal, mainly due to what will be discussed in the next paragraph.

Religion:

I was indoctrinated into a certain religion since I was a fetus. I am being very serious in saying this, literally from the womb. I had high expectations from a very young age. I was expected not to indulge in any celebrations, I was expected not to act like the other kids ever since I was in preschool. This was easy in preschool; I would explain my beliefs, and no one would question. (Why would we? we were 3-5 years old.) As I got older though, people became annoyed at my limitations and lack of participation. They would roll their eyes constantly at me. As part of my religion, I was not allowed to go to sleepovers, parties, or anything outside of school. You can imagine how isolating that felt, knowing that EVERYONE else was hanging out and having a great time, while I was stuck at home alone. I was expected to be a good example; my parent's friend called me "the perfect child." It seems like the other kids in my religion (note that there are very few, again isolating) get a little leeway, a little slack. Not me. Not at all. It scares me so bad, knowing that if I express normal human desires; like a desire for sex or love, I will be criticized, and my feelings invalidated. My parent will take everything away from me, every distraction I need that keeps me alive, and once again I will spiral. I am scared of how everyone else will react if I mess up as well, these people have seen me grow up. They know how well behaved I was when I was young. I am scared of how they will feel if I mess up. The scariest thing is when you're elevated so high, if you were to fall, it will be lethal. Not to mention, everyone (with the exception of 3) of the members of my religion are adults. I have no one my age to relate to. I am constantly surrounded by mature people, (I have been ever since my birth, which is why I matured early and was different from other kids, I never really understood them, like I should have). As a group, we hardly EVER do things together, almost as if we are classmates who spend an hour and a half together, but never truly try to get to know one another. So, imagine this; I don't get to have friends in school, and I am constantly surrounded by mature ADULTS my whole life. Sounds fun, doesn't it? Throughout these years I found solace on the internet, especially YouTube. However, this oasis would quickly turn to an addiction.

Addictions:

Most people have experience with an addiction from the ages of 18-25. I battled with one from the age of 5-15. (Still a minor btw.) I'll let you decide whether that is depressing. The addiction in question was YouTube and Tv. I made friends with the Youtubers who would never know who I am. What was I supposed to do? Stare at the walls of my room? I did what I could to fill the void (it hasn't been filled to this day).  It worked in the moment, but it always wears off, then there's the need to fill it up all over again. A vicious cycle that I lived with for more than a decade. I have dermatillomania. (Look it up). also have an addiction to chewing toilet paper. (I am aware of the unorthodox nature of this). This is a habit I still cannot shake. I heard its called Pica (idk) I've also started c*tting recently. (I know I know it's bad, but I hurt so I have to release since I have no dr*gs or alchoh*l this is the next option. Since when I journal, my parent finds it and gets batshit crazy about the contents. If i had any more addictions, I think I'd be addicted to alcohol and p*rn. (If I had access) Of course, my parent has no idea of this, I would like to keep it this way. As they are extreme. 

Abuse:

I have encountered physical and verbal abuse. In preschool a teacher would be unnecessarily rough with me. (How much of a fight could a three-year old put up that you would need to be that rough?) She was an adult woman btw. She would push me into chairs and be really mean. I remember one instance when she would pick me up and underneath my armpits would hurt tremendously. At the time I thought I was heavy. But that proved to be untrue, since later in my years she picked me up and it didn't hurt at all. Not one bit. So, this was obviously a choice to hurt a 3–5-year-old. I'm not sure why this doesn't bother me now that I'm older, I feel like maybe I'm being dramatic and maybe this isn't abuse. If i were to actually know if this were abuse, maybe I'd feel something about it. Moving onto the verbal abuse; this came from someone you're supposed to run to for comfort. The possessor of hard candies and granny slippers. It's grandma, but not the sweet granny that everyone seems to have. No No, this granny was the verbally abusive one who hurts everybody in the room. Who would tell you that "you deserve to have bad things happen to you." Who would curse you out and threaten to ban you from her house because you were playing a game.  She has a history of this, with her own children but never her siblings. She will hurt everyone who are kin to her, but never those who shared a womb with her.  My parent has experienced this and so has my grandmother's other child. Everyone around her, has been hurt in one way or another. She is quite toxic, but here is the kicker, she is not willing to change for ANYONE. So, this behavior will continue because she doesn't love us enough to stop hurting us. How nice. 

Exposure to sex:

I was exposed to sex at a very early age, unfortunately for me this also meant I engaged in it at an early age as well. I won't go into details because I am still recovering from it. But, now I am hypersexual unfortunately as a coping mechanism. What a childhood! (before the age of 13) Most of it spent alone and worrying about whether I was pregnant! How fun!! (sarcasm)  (I wasn't aware that my organs hadn't developed for that yet.) and  (If I'm  going to be honest I don't remember my childhood much at all). 

Su*cide 

I've had experiences with depression, anxiety and ptsd all before the age of 16. I'm not sure if that's normal or not but whatever. I had depression one summer and never really recovered from that. I plan on dying at 22. I only have a few more years until then. Am I scared? Sometimes, but in a twisted way I think death is beautiful. It's pure, doesn't discriminate and is painless. The utter lack of everything is overpowering and beautiful to me. Anyway, my first attempt was when I was 7. I simply tried to suffocate myself by not breathing. This obviously didn't work. 

 I would also like to include a letter I wrote a few years ago to my hypothetical therapist I would see. The letter begins with the following quotations;

"Dear Therapist, 
My name is , and the reason I am writing is because I have many issues that I need to work out and I was hoping you could help me. Where to begin with my few problems? Well, the only logical thing to do is to start at the beginning. I was born with only one parent; it was hard and sometimes a bit lonely but we both dealt with it. My dad stayed in his hometown and my mom moved to California, I am incredibly happy the way things turned out because I love living with my mom. I do not really like to talk to my dad because, I just do not feel like talking to him for some reason. I will not reveal too much about him because he is not really a bad person. Anyway, my biggest problem is my confidence, I have extremely low confidence and self-esteem, this is because people in school during the fourth grade called me fat and dumb. But the biggest place where I have absolutely no confidence is in mathematics, I do not understand it, it is not easy and I refrain from asking for help, the way I see it is: I set my standards low so when they drop they don’t drop by very much, and this works at least 50% of the time, the other 50% of the time when I actually think I can master it, 20% of the time I can but the rest I fail .  My social status has taken a few hits as well as my ego (which was not big to begin with) In school people excluded me from their games and etcetera, I would often beg them to let me join, and I have gotten so used to this I would shun other people because I wanted to join the group so badly. There is one problem I had that sent me to the principal’s office, there was a new girl that I wanted to know better, but everyone in my class was just hovering over her and they would not even let me talk to her. So, this went on for quite a few days and I finally snapped I screamed to a girl who was talking to her “I’m going to kill you!” of course I meant it jokingly but even now I know that was terribly misinterpreted. And it was terribly uncalled for I spent the rest of the week talking about it with the principal, one thing you must know about me before you write notes in your note pad is that I am easily angered, I well do not get along with others. That has changed, I am no longer a social butterfly, ever since this pandemic I have no longer wanted the company of people, if there is a choice for me to either be alone or be with people, I would choose to be alone. This pandemic has made me realize one key thing about myself: The only people I need are  SSSniperwolf, Jaiden animations, SSSundee, and Alex Meyers. (all of them are youtubers) They know how to tickle my funny bone, not people! Again, I spend a lot of time either on YouTube or the internet. I have never really had to rely on ANYONE BUT MYSELF. And I don’t like it, I am sad mad most of the time, (it is a combination of anger and sadness and frustration) And I can be depressed sometimes. I get nervous around other people, I get nervous about how I look, I feel like everyone is judging me (for some weird reason) but people have better things to do than to judge me, they have lives to live. But I still get nervous. Sometimes I get so anxious and nervous I make myself sick. I have social anxiety. I also think I am annoying and automatically assume everyone else thinks so to.  If I had a choice to be with people or not (again) I would choose not to. I tend to embarrass myself when I am with people ( more so with my family) , so I think it is just best to avoid them. I’m tired of embarrassing myself and feeling bad for existing. So I just avoid FAMILY AND FRIENDS. But my biggest problem is that I DON’T LIKE PEOPLE! I do not trust them, I think people are corrupt and selfish I think people are mean and liars, with some exceptions (my family) I prefer to be by myself because I will not lie or exclude myself like that girl did. People make me nervous, you really never know what to expect. Also, my school experience before the pandemic was not exceptionally good, some teachers were mean and gave me a hard time (quite frequently) and my preschool was not very good at all. Preschool was hard because this teacher would squeeze me awfully hard and yell and push me into chairs. There was this one instance where I took off my shoes to scratch my foot and she saw my foot and said, “put your shoe on!” but at the time I did not know how, and the shoes was tied tight.  I was like four or five, so I tried to jam it in, I only got it halfway through and I thought she would not notice. But when she did, she threw me in a chair and snatched my foot to put it in her lap and tied my shoe then let my foot drop to the ground. I tried hard to help around the class, I helped out around the classroom. But I guess she did not like me. I do not know why. But I still liked her and wanted to give her hugs. Now when I was in fifth grade my classmates were on a fieldtrip and I stayed at school because my mom could not go, now I was paired with my fourth-grade teacher. Once I got there, she said the class was going to make clothing out of recycled items, me my friend started to get excited and started to get cloth, then she walked over and asked, “What are you doing?!” we said we were making a dress, and she said “no, no, you are helping the fourth graders make them.” In a rude tone. So, we walked around. I walked around and asked everyone if they needed help. No one said yes, so I stepped back and just let them work and if anyone needed help, I was available, well the teacher came up and said “go help someone” I said that no one needed help. but I walked away. Well, I finally found someone who needed help and helped them start to make the costume. Well, the teacher walks up and says, “What are you doing, where is your sketch?”  bear in mind she said NOTHING about a sketch. So, I quickly drew up a sketch, and continued to work, she came up again and said, “where is your work?!” I showed her the sketch and she said “no, no this isn’t a sketch” she got us so upset that she made a little kid run to the bathroom and cry! There are more stories with this horrible harpy, I will only share one more, I was in fourth grade with the same teacher( we will not call her by name, we will call her  Ms. Meanie) Now “Ms. Meanie” told us of an assignment we were supposed to do, (we were to make board games of the books we were reading) me and my group got right to work we were really motivated, we started making the characters, and the board, well I can’t really remember in great detail what happened but I do know this, she stressed us all out and made things worse, we ripped up our entire board and started over on the other side, when she saw that we were ripping it she got frustrated and just made us feel bad. She is a monster, I would prefer she be locked up than ever be in her class again, I want her to feel how much sadness and anger and frustration she brought to me as a kid. Whoever thought she was qualified to teach was a madman, she obviously had no patience at all, if I can stop this from happening to any other kid that would make me feel better. She obviously is not particularly good with children at all, she is impatient, moody, phony, short tempered, vain, smug, relentless, and plain rude. As you can see school was not an exceptionally good place, but I still loved going, and still do. But it still hurts me to look at my school yearbook. My teachers were very mean, one in preschool, was PHYSICALLY abusive. While all the others were verbally and emotionally abusive. I still wake up with nightmares and feelings of immense anger and frustration, I feel as if I am confined to respect the teachers, and I feel as if I can never tell them how terrible they made me feel. It has gotten so bad that I made plans to not talk to ANYONE or do ANYTHING ( at recess) at school so I do not get In trouble because I know that the teacher will always win the argument. And other teachers will automatically just side with the teacher. It is a corrupt system, what even is the point of it all?! Since it is all the same. Teachers make you feel bad, you try to talk and they cut you off like the vicious, judgmental, cruel, machete-mouthed bitches they are. (Sorry for the language) I wake up and scream at the because I have dreams about the scenarios that happened at school. It is that bad. What did I do to deserve all the rudeness?!  I tried to help other people, I was kind: I helped a teacher who was afraid of spider’s cope, I was generous: I donated whatever the school asked to the many causes and fundraisers they had. s. I was helpful: I hugged people often.  I was friendly: I played with people that no one else wanted to play with.  What do I get in return? Backstabbed, scorned, abused, resented, left out, ridiculed, bullied, and so much more. I also have a few mental problems; I have severe anxiety and worry (and social anxiety). I also have feelings of anger because I want to tell that teacher so bad how I feel. I also have this thing called excoriation disorder. I believe it is a skin picking disorder. My last problem is that I have is Sometimes I’m so lonely I make up scenarios in my head so that I have someone to talk to. I pretend that people that I think are cool are there so I have someone to talk to. But ii usually just end up talking to myself. I end up realizing that no one is there and I am talking to myself 
  SO anyway doctor, I was hoping you could help heal the many wounds inflicted on my person by the society. "

I wrote this when I was 12. 

Anyway thanks for letting me vent :)

On Wednesday morning, everything was fine between my mother and me. It's just the two of us, but our relationship has always been complicated. I haven't been exactly an easy daughter (but I've never been involved in any major problems, like teenage pregnancy, drugs or even crime). She asked me if I thought she was a good mother and I found the question strange, so unexpected. I insisted and she ended up admitting that she thinks I would be capable of harming her in some way. (This is due to the mysterious disappearance of a porcelain bowl that I didn't even know existed, since I don't touch that kind of thing of hers - the only things I touch are craft supplies, I very rarely borrow any of her clothes and I let her know when I do, and everyday things, like... what's in the kitchen.) After hearing this absurd question from her, I felt terrible to the point of almost giving up on my plans (which, by the way, involved her) and questioning my own ability.

I'm not working at the moment, so I spend more time at home than she does. Today, I ended up exploding and she implied that she believed I had taken the piece and sold it, saying that she had no doubt that I was capable of it. The thing is that I don't take a single cent from her without asking first. And until recently, if I needed, say, a dollar bill, I would take it and replace it with coins that would be exactly (sometimes even a few cents more, never less) the same amount I had taken. Even when I do something I shouldn't (not necessarily something serious), I feel guilty and end up admitting it. So, yeah. I am pretty honest with my mother for most of the time (The only thing I haven't told her - and because I know what she's going to do - is how much I'm saving. My plan was to invest this money in an auction license and, as a result, give her a better life. These savings are solely and exclusively from sales I've made, but from toys that belonged to a client's son, things I bought to resell, like dolls, books and even some old magazines that I have. And also from an app that pays a cent for each registered invoice and they have a minimum of US$ 1.74 to withdraw. If she finds out about this money, she'll want me to use it for other purposes, and honestly, that's not something I want. I mean, like I said, I don't have a job. I'm trying really hard to save each and any penny that I get. There were temptations along the way, but it's been good because it's forcing me to save. I have set a significant goal, so I am really trying hard not to spend and most importantly: Achieve that sum.) and that accusation seriously hurt and humiliated me. Deeply. I think we have finally reached a point of no return and I see no other way out than to get away from her as soon as possible.

I want to feel secure in my skin. To feel like like my body is fine as it is. To feel like I'm fine where I am as I try to reach my goals- of weight loss, of better health, of security. Yet it's hard to stay present. It's hard to want to be connected to the fat body I have, when I yearn for the 'thinner' future I want, even as I try to rephrase it as healthy. Deep down, no matter how many times I rephrase it as healthy, as a lifestyle change, and even if the very steps themselves I know for a FACT are not disordered eating- deep down, my mind still tries to label it as thin.

It's funny to me how people often think medication for eating disorders like binge eating means that everything will be easier. In a way, kind of, I feel hunger normally, food... doesn't hold as addictive as a pull like a drug, but, it doesn't change the behaviors, or that my mind still kind of yearns for it in hard times. Yearns for it, but then, that 'high' you expected isn't there and it's... kind of scary in a strange way. Scary cause I no longer have the same control I used to and I don't entirely know what to do.

Yet I feel numb. I want to eat but I feel nothing from it. It's not giving me that high, and I get sick from smaller portions like normal people would. It's... strange. I'm still not used to it. I'm not sure I'll ever be. It's so damn hard to feel secure in my skin and even though I know this will pass, fuck do I wish this would pass faster. I wish whatever the fuck this feeling was that was sitting heavy, numbing me, a rock in my chest would just come off so I could cough it out already.

I hate so much this waiting, waiting for this THING to crawl out so I can process it.

my (16f) partner (15) came out as trans ftm in December. Before that, we were in a lesbian relationship for 2 years, but we are both bisexual so there was no immediate reason to break it off. I have been living in another country since January and am returning home in June. This means we have only had contact over the phone in the last few months, but we text a lot so we are close. It is still really hard for me tod eal with having a boyfriend. In the beginning I felt like I lost my identity, because being gay was an important part of it which I just don't feel anymore. But I came to terms with that and continued to be happy. But now, 5 months later, it suddenly hits me again and everything about it is so hard.

Reality. I feel like the the world around me can't be real. Even when I know it is, it seems fake. Like I don't really think I can die. I KNOW I can, but it doesn't seem like a real thing to me. Even though I almost have died a few times, in reality. I don't know how to explain it.

Hi, Im a radom teen in his 20s, sorry for my bad english, it's not my first language, thanks for listening to this.
I don't much friends, basically 2 groups of friends of about 7 people each. Let's call them group A and group B. I've known A for at least 3 years now, I've never talked much, I've never really felt as if I fit, they've always said that they accepted me as another friend of them, but even with that I just feel as a burden, as someone who shouldn't really be there. For example, when we do sleepovers they usually drink and play drinking games, I don't drink so I just sit there, watching them having fun, which doesn't really upset me, not until they start talking about things, usually sex related things for some reason, and not only I can't relate or enjoy listening to them, but also the make it so terrible to me,  they may ask or mention things about me that I'm not comfortable with, and even after saying that I don't feel good when they do that, they still do that kind of things, even when im not present. That's just one example, but there's a lot more, from literally not being even considered in 6 hours of meeting, to not saying a single word in a full day. I have to add that when I'm with them I have to try very hard to have interactions, and I end up really tired.
On the other hand we have B. I haven't known them for much time, just a couple of months. This group is smaller, lets says 4 people, which are (fake names) Anna, Beth, Jude and John. I've known Anna for some years, because she is the best friend of a good friend of mine, but we hadn't really talked much in the 3/4 years we've known of each other's existance, I didn't know the rest. This year, when starting to study in a new place, Anna and I picked the same train to study in the same place. I saw her, but I didn't know if I should've said something to her, so I didn't, but she came up to me, very kindly, and asked me to join her and her group of friends to chat and stuff. I inmediatly clicked with them. Since I've known them I kinda have grown very fond of them, we get along really really well. When I'm with them Ihave a warm feeling of belonging that I've never felt before. It's as if we were meant to be friends since forever. I've been having meeting with them, together and one by one, a lot this last 2 months, and I've been incredibly glad about it, but I don't really hang out much with group A since, at least, summer.
What's the issue then? you may be asking, well, let me give you a bit more of context. I have a partner, since 3 years ago, who is also in group A. This relationship I must say that is very toxic, from both sides, I'm a fucking dumbass, the worst boyfriend ever, I'm not made for relationships, and my partner is destroying my mental health and manipulating me. I hate to say it this way, I don't like to victimize myself, but sadly it is this way. I genuinely can't get out of this relationship, I've tried, and every time I end up coming back, feeling like shit, saying sorry for things I haven't even made. My partner has said things about our relationship to group A, but everything they know about is onlly the things I've done bad, every single one. I asked my partner to at least not to tell them anything personal about my life that I haven't said to them, they didn't listen to me and ended up telling more things that what I'd like them to know of (things like my attemp of suicide,my problems with other people, with my parents, etc..)
All of this adds up to why I feel "unwanted" or at least uncomfortable with group A but not with group B.
My partner has asked me several times why I don't hang out with A but I do with B. I told them why and how i felt towards group A, but now they think I kinda hate A but "overlove" B. They said that A wanted to see me more often, that they cared about me but didnt know how to ask about how I was feeling because I don't share anything with them. I told my partner that if they wanted to know anything about me they should ask me themselves, not my partner, that they should at least try to (for example) talk to me when we hang out and so on, but my partner got angry, defending that they were trying as hard as possible while everything I do is complain. That made me feel like trash, as if wasn't really triying to interact, to be a good friend, but I know I'm trying. That conversation ended up with  my partner saying that they would make sure to make them ask me personally why i don't want to see them. 
Twomorrow we celebrates the birthday of a member of group A, we will all sleep in her house, all except me, because everything I said earlier, to be honest I don't even want to go for the day, but they made me feel really bad for it, so I accepted to go. 
I have a big fear about twomorrow, I fear they "face me" all at the same time and I have to tell them all of this, and I fear even more that they say something along the lines of "You prefer B befpre us why do you hate us or anything"
This is seriously killing me, among a lot of other things, but right this one is very terrible right now.
I've talked to Anna and Beth separately about this, and they both said that it was kind of a shitty way to treat me, that I shouldn't be worried because I didn't do anything wrong, but I still fear it.
To make matters worse I don't even know how I really feel towards Anna and Beth, not in a bad way, in a "Am I having romantic feeling towards this person or do I just want to be very good friends with them" and it's driving me crazy. As friends I do love them, they are the sweetest, kindest and coolest persons I know of, but I fear falling in love with any of them. 
I don¡t even know what to do, what to feel or even how to sleep thinking about all of this. I'm going completely crazy. 
Thank you for listening to me talking about my shit, even with my bad way of epressing al telling everything, there's obviously more context, but I don't want to be another 6hours talking about it. Thank you very much, this really means a lot to me.

Can I get opinions: 
Do people find nicknames more for close personal relationships and if its not there stick to more standard title?
So one grandparent gave himself his own nickname for all the grandkids to call him. 
All the other grandkids have always called him that. He has always been part of their lives since they were babies. Even when the majority bulk moved out of state he would go out of his way to go visit them all the time. 
Husband and I finally after a decade of struggling to have children finally were blessed with 2 children. My first born he met only 3 times  one of which we went to them(shes almost 4) and my second born (about to turn 2) he has never even met at all. We used to ask all the time about coming to visit. It was always excuses, busy with another set of grandkids in their state and in yet he had time to come down to the same state we live in as his other grandkids to visit them. He came down a total, that I know of, 4 different times for them. We are clearly seeing the favoritism and it just hurts my heart as a parent that our children dont have the grandparents that care or willing to put in the effort like they do for the others. I was blessed to have grandparents there who cared and loved me and gave me great childhood memories with them I was really hoping it would've been the same for our kids and it hurts that they have no village other then me and their dad.
Unfortunately, we feel obligated to give them xmas this year. Im sure when we get there hes going to expect my kids to call him by his nickname. Personally I feel nicknames are special. They should be for people to use only in your close circle or super close loved ones one who are always in your life. Hes choosing not be in our kids. So I just feel we should stick to technicality and just have the kids say the term "grandpa". 
What do yall think? Is it a petty thought?

It makes me so afraid of everything and don't tell me it's just anxiety because I know it's not. I have lived with anxiety for so long and I know what that feels like, but this, this is different. I've come to realize that the real reason I stayed with the people that abused me, was not because of love for them but for the guilt I felt towards them. Since I was a child, I had a big heart. One too full of love for a world full of people like this. I always tried to help people, even when I knew I couldn't save them. I knew many people who had no hope and I never understood why. I tried my hardest to help but felt an overbearing guilt on my shoulders for the people I couldn't help, having to watch them fall down so far. I thought I had done everything right. I helped as much as I could, I pushed myself to do more everyday, I let them do as they pleased to me just to help them feel better. In the end, I was always pushed aside. That guilt from the lack of help I was able to provide since I was only a child, did affect me and my relationships today. I always feel guilt, no matter what I do. That guilt I feel everyday led me to allowing abuse from old partners in order to make up for the lack of help I could've gave people. I understand why most had no hope left now. I understand. This feeling of dread. I understand it now. Though, I've come to understand that It also wasn't my fault. I was  a kid, I just had a heart full of too much love for people. I couldn't understand that people can be cruel. I didn't understand that my help wasn't going to be enough for some of those people, they needed more help, help that wasn't from a child. They shouldn't have told me how they were mentally, I was too young to help, I had my own problems and they were old enough to know they should've spoken to a doctor or someone close in age as them.
I understand it was never my fault.
The guilt I feel is normal to feel but it is not something I have to carry,
It wasn't my fault.
I was a sweethearted kid and I still am though now I understand, I am not like a god or a doctor 
I am just a teenage girl now, who has her own problems. I can help to the best of my ability but
I will never give myself up again for people who never wanted the help.

My friend in high school had a pretty bad situation at her home. Her mother and elder sister teamed up against her, and there was a lot of verbal abuse. She was a bright student, 95 and above always. But she wasn't always smart, she was pretty weak in junior grades, and had worked very hard to become a bright student in high school. But our class was pretty competitive and she still was in the top 20-30 students, so her mother always forced her to try and do better, because her sister was always the topper (ofc years back her sister didn't had that much competition she had to face) During summer vacation I kinda lost touch with her. In the last week she called me to ask a weird question - "how do you cover up something from your parents? Like if it is kinda attached  to you...your body" At first I couldn't quite guess what she meant. But then I realized. She was harming her self. I tried to console her to stop hiding her wounds from her family, talked her with the school counselor- you know trying to do the apt stuff. Her mother found out and, yeah her mother was kinda weird- she started blaming her for everything and all. Took a toll on her. She confided in me. I tried to talk her to the school counselor and all, didn't help. I didn't want to share her personal stuff to a lot of people, and being in high school, I couldn't be her best counselor myself. Also I am kinda stupid with words. I don't think much while speaking. I couldn't be the support she needed and all this took a toll on her. She died by hanging herself. Yeah it was actually second time I have lost a close friend of mine, I was quiet regretful.. but I thought it was her family. I did my best as a friend. 
Months prior, I had changed schools and all, I was gonna deactivate my previous school Gmail ID (we were allotted specific school email ids during pandemic for online classes and stuff) I found a email from a username that my late friend often used online. It was about a week prior to her death. 
She confessed about her problems and all. 
Her family disliked me. Since she continued being my friend, her family also criticized her for a poor choice in friends and blamed everything wrong with her as a fault of her friend circle (which was me and like 2 other girls). She disliked me too. She confessed how everybody had a liking towards me, I once cried during lunch and everyone in class thought it was because of her. I didn't knew about this. She didn't wanna be friends with me- because well, I had a pretty dirty humor sometimes. And because I was hindrance in her studies as I performed better in languages than her even though she worked harder than me (because in languages I know how to write an apt answer as to satiate the teacher where as she was the type to write essays of philosophy and a bit too intelligent stuff)... She wrote that I was a bully in denial. *You never took my problems seriously, all you did was go confess it to the counselor for gossip. You showed sympathy just to look good. You gave me a bad cold and hot attachment to yourself. I try to get away from you but then I know there is no one else to be around. I cry and I come back to you*. 
Apparently since everybody had started to dislike her since they put me in a good light and her in a bad light for making me cry (she didn't), and because she had a problematic reputation with due to cuts on her arms- it was obvious she could never make other friends. So, she confessed feeling trapped in my friendship. Not wanting to be friends, but didn't want to be alone either. And the whole Gmail end with a "I don't hate you. Once we graduate we don't have to ever talk again. I hope next year I could change schools and get away. It's not your fault, I don't want you to repent. I wish a happy life to you and your family."
Well that was a lot to digest at that time. Maybe if I read this Gmail confession prior to her death, we could talk it out? But I wasn't the sole reason she killed herself? But yeah there was hope. Maybe she was asking for her help one last time. I do have tried to change myself a lot. I don't humor with people anymore. Indeed I have started to have a great disgust with funny people. I don't have casual friendships anymore. I only talk to people about academic stuff and all. And I feel like a better person. Even though I don't accept I was a total bad person in the past. I was a teenager. I was immature. I couldn't have dealt with her problems wisely anyhow. It was stupid of her too to have high expectations from me. Maybe if she questioned if truly I was doing this on purpose, or maybe if I was just a stupid child brained too who couldn't possibly help her. She should have confided in someone better. We both have faults to share, which I feel we both acknowledged. 
As for her family? They are obviously devastated. They have the right to be. Her mother is seeking therapy from a professional psychiatrist, which is I don't know, quite ironical? But I do feel bad for her. Of course deep in her heart she will always be guilty. Her sister is currently out of the country and I know she must think about her. They never had a great sibling dynamic, but I refuse to believe she never shed tears for her- unlike me.
 I could never cry. Is it guilt that I don't feel deserving to tear for her? Is it I am truly a bad person and never cared of her as a friend? Is it because I am angry for her decision? Is it because I feel she is undeserving of my tears? Is it because I could never cry for a girl I loved and couldn't confess? Is it because it's to late to confess?
I don't know. After all I am a narcissist in denial.

Its like, right, cuz ik all my friends would totally be cool with referring to me w he him and stuffs in a heartbeat, but id never let that happen to me, because that would mean that what i want could actually be possible. Its just, its like one of my fantasies that ive accepted that its impossible. Same as how I will always and always be annoying despite all my efforts to become otherwise (laziness, emotional, fat and somewhat distasteful), as well as being incapable of love, and being, well. And having people actually to see me for who i am feel like i am actually one step closer to becoming who i wish to be, and I know that is factually impossible, so I would never let myself feel hope just for it to become futile. I wanted to say so hard that I’ve tried my best to be the daughter that my mom wanted, but I was never really. I feel like every waking second for me is a second I feel should be reserved for someone else, and I do not deserve the opportunities that I have had. And to accept my irl identity as a trans guy feel like I am just turning my fear of never being good enough for my family closer to a reality than just a irrational fear. In the same vain, I would never ever think of myself as suicidal, because I actually love life and I feel like I dont have a good reason to think about ending it all ever. But theres just like, an unrivaled belief in the back of my mind: that ill never make it past 25. Like, Im not depressed or something, its just a truth that ill never live and get married and make childrens, all that shit, like society wanted (im asian btw lol), so what do i get out of living that long?

I've done nothing yet everything wrong at the same time. When it's at it's worst, I really feel like I deserve the pain that haunts me for why else would it be so visceral, so constant? There has to be a reason, even though I don't want there to be. So I tell myself I'm at my fault. It's my body, my personality, me that has brought upon this monstrous wave of despair. I am the wave, and I am the ocean. Just one flowing body of nothingness that occasionally gets rocked into bouts of despair and anger but returns to emptiness, always. And I am the problem. Not just my body or my weight or the way I act or the way I talk. Me, at my core, is a terrible thing. A monster with claws and fangs and a shriveled-up thing that doesn't conform to any gender or name or concept other than destruction. That monster inside me is all that I am, and the body that shields is but simply a shield for other's eyes to see.

Why can't my parents accept that I can't do online college? Ever since the shutdown of my high school in my Senior year due to the pandemic, I struggled miserably to finish the year. I was a "gifted" kid, known to be one of the more capable/hardworking students, and I was proud of that... but everything came crashing down. I was so unmotivated; it was a miracle I even graduated given all the missing assignments I had.
I was accepted into a university that promised in-person classes, but it was just a fancy way of saying "Hey, we're allowing you to stay at our campus dorms, but your classes are all online!" Surprisingly, I was okay at it. Mainly because I was surrounded by other students who could keep me accountable to work hard. I had to take the next semester from home and I failed two of my three classes, just BARELY passing the third one.
After that, the family moved to another state and I had to come with them, so I had to withdraw from the university. There weren't many college/university options that offered a bachelor's degree (which my parents are insisting I get or else I'm a failure in their eyes) that were deemed acceptable. SO GUESS WHAT? Online-exclusive college! I went to one and actually FAILED five of the six classes. I was then placed under academic suspension.
What is my parents' solution to this? FIND ANOTHER ONLINE COLLEGE! I'm so sick of this. I'm already feeling like I'm failing my first class. Do I really have no say in this? I feel so belittled - like my opinions never matter. I'm of goddamn DRINKING AGE and I can't even make any decisions; I'm not even allowed to leave the house to go for a walk around the block!!!!!

I'm 17, the same age of the kid my dad beat to death, the guilt eats at me. 

My dad's always been a man prone to violence, sometimes beating his kids as a form of punishment. My brother is now 26, but when he was 21 he was struggling with drugs. My dad would tell him how he was a nobody, how he was always going to be a nobody and how he was a waste of space. One night my dad got physical with him and my brother tried to fight back. 

The whole event is a blur to me, but it instilled such a fear in me that I'd avoid my dad every chance I'd get and I'd flinch every time I was near him.
My dad noticed my behavior, got mad at me and tried to have a conversation about it. His point was that violence was sometimes necessary and that's when he confessed to 12yo me his wrongdoings, justifying them.

He was in his early 30's, working in construction sites, when he met a 17yo kid, who used to steal shit from the sites so he could buy drugs. When my dad and his buddies managed to catch him, they pinned him to the ground and took turns beating him, with the intention of punishing him. They left the kid there and he died. Nothing came of it, the police didn't look into it.

I wonder if when he goes to church, when he beats his children, when he lives another year, when he gives a ride to the neighbor's kid that's in and out of rehab, I wonder if he thinks about that kid. 

My brother is doing good: he's sober, has a really good job, a girlfriend and I barely see him anymore. He doesn't know about this.

Hi. This a vent about everything thing that happened recently and just how I've been feeling. The past week were really bad. They were really chaotic with school starting and mom having problems with the car, my grandparents being.. Themselves, not in a good way, and me getting sick often. Honestly I'm starting to think I've been just floating through everything. I'm just so tired. Of everything. I've lost goals and motivation, and i struggle with energy and everything feels like a never ending impossible task. It's exhausting. It's been getting physicall as well. Recently I've also been getting sick more often, i got high fever for week or so, twice in the span of two months. i often have stomach pain, head and throat ache and i struggle with sleep, i already did before but now it's getting worse. Everything is physically and mentally tiring no matter what it is, and i often get light headed and physical pain every where. This all obviously affects my performance in school which is terrible. It's only the first part of the year so it makes sense that the grades are lower than I'd like them to be. But i still feel like a horrible. I still feel like a dissapoinment. Because people used to say that i have potential and that I'm very smart. And i hate to disappoint them. But it's not true. I'm actually very stupid. I just memorize things decently well and decently fast, I'm not sure how useful that is but it is what is. Recently I've been trying everything to improve things. On all sides. I've been trying to improve my diet, I've been trying to set up good habits and a better routine, I've been trying to be more punctual. I've been trying to take care of my appearance to feel more put together. I've trying to take plenty of notes and store them neatly. I've been trying everything to fix things. But i can't. I do make progress but it's very small. It's like making a step forward and 2 backwards. So it's useless. I still feel tired. I've been feeling this way for years. And recently I've only been feeling more and more anxious and overwhelmed. I don't know what to do. I don't know how to fix it. Also if my mother doesn't already want me dead i know for sure she's so close. I know she's right. I'm lazy. And stupid. I have been doing less chores 'to concentrate' on homework but my still grades aren't as good as I'd like. Recently I'm pretty messed up and in my thoughts because of everything and she's been getting mad at me more often, we don't really argue most of the time. It's just her yelling and me listening in silence. She rarely listens to me normally, she does not even consider letting me speak when she's mad. But I'm wrong anyway, so there's no point. I just kinda wish she'd tell me things. Normally. Without yelling. And just ask me for help or to do chores. I don't understand why she doesn't. It's not like I'm going to say no. So why can't she ask me? And yeah. I know. I should be responsible and do them with her asking me but that would envolve being close to her more than usual and that kinda makes me uncomfortable. If i do things spontaneously by myself she makes comments about how i finally did something useful. I know she's right but it still doesn't feel nice. Also she said I'm never around and never spend time with her but i always kinda felt like she doesn't actually want me around. Even as a kid she didn't really want me around and getting her to play with me or stay together and watch movies was a struggle, and she completely stopped over the years. I know she was busy, and i know i was a horrible child but it still didn't feel nice. When i try to help her she always says that I'm stupid and not capable of doing things right, she gets mad and doesn't let me help and sends me away. I wonder, how can i learn to do those things she wants me to learn from her and practice them when she doesn't let me do anything? All this stuff just makes me feel uncomfortable around her. And now prefer to do things by myself, where no one can judge me or make comments about how i do things. And I've realized i keep a lot of secrets from her. More than I'd want to and basically lie a daily basis about small things. It's wrong i know but the though of telling her the truth and getting comments and insults when everything is already so messed up makes me wanna puke. Lying and hiding in my room while i figure this whole thing out is much easier.

I always live on constant fear. "What if I did something wrong? What if my mistake affects my future? My family? My friends? Group mates? The one put trust on me?"
I can't afford mistake, yet I don't change
I can change, but I don’t change
I play games, video games
That way, I will forget my fear for just a moment
But when I stop playing, I realize that was just a mistake I made
By spending time to ignore my fear, it gets worse
Because I'm scared of disappoiting people
I'm scared that maybe I make others think that they should never trust me
That's why in all activity I can do, I like to sleep
I used to love to play more than to sleep, but now it’s the opposite
everyone can and need to sleep
So I can sleep without pretending not running from my fear
So that I could live for another 6 hours without thinking
The moment I wake up, I will have to deal with reality again, so I don’t want to wake up
People ask if one day, I can choose to sleep forever in a simulation, would I do it?
I want to, but I would not be able to say it, because I'm scared that decision will affect everyone too
I'm scared of choosing
maybe I have been protected by my mother for so long that I forget how to choose
One day, when she died, she will eventually die, as everyone will die, will I choose to end myself?
I don't know, and wish to never know
Maybe I'm a lazy person, and I will not deny it.
I wish I have a switch that can turn on and off my emotion, so that I can be free from my fear.
I wish to be able to turn off my emotion for just a while, for an hour or two, could be three or four if I can, so that I will not let my laziness affect myself and others.
I wish I can bring happiness to everyone I love.
I wish that even if I die, nobody will be affected negatively by me.
I never think my existence is a mistake
My mother didn’t make a mistake
It is not a mistake for being born
I am the one make mistakes
And because of me, my mother will be sad
I’m not smart, or bright, or friendly
I’m stubborn, lazy, coward, and being an overdramatic guy
My mental health is normal, and I’m just being lazy
Yes
That’s right
I’m always lazy
Sleeping on my own fake success as a kid and can’t wake up
Is it too late?
Probably not
It’s never too late
I’m the one choose not changing myself
It is because of me
It’s always me
My fault all along
Not my parents, not my living condition
Not my mental health or whatever
It is my choice to be a failure
And I am the only one resposible for it
I have a lovely family
My father is not strict, and while he’s usually out for working, we still have good time together when we have chances
My mother is a lovable person. She spends time with me. She teaches me many subjects at home, like chemistry and physics. She spends time hearing my problem, and she is the one that cares about me most. Yes, way more than my father
My brother, I can’t say he is a good son, but we also play together when I was young. We don’t talk much anymore, but we don’t have negative attitude toward each other
I live in a nice house. Not a big or luxury one, but enough for us to live comfortably together
I finished elementary school, secondary school, then high school. Not with flying color, but still quite good
I got in a good university thanks to effort (as my friend said) and luck
Do I have low esteem? Probably
I failed a lot
To the point my heart beats like crazy everytime I have a test, no matter big or small
My mother doesn’t yell at me, just looked at me disappointed
She didn’t say, but I know
She advised me gently
But I’m still sad
Maybe if I worked a bit harder, she would look at me with proud eyes
She loves me, I know
She always said so, and she shows it through her action
But my mother’s health is not good
She gave me a lot of advice in case she is gone before she could teach it
I hate those times
I hate to imagine her death
I hate that she will die before me
So I wish
I wish that she doesn’t have to look at me with worrying eyes when she dies
I wish that I could be a better person
I wish that if she dies before that, I will die the day after her, in my sleep. So that’s no one blame my death to her.
I don’t have many things that keep me alive
My mother is one of very few of them
I don’t have a hobby, I don’t have close friends, and I don’t think I love my father as much as my mother. I still love him, but he just doesn’t spend that much time with me
I wish for a painless and quiet death.
But as long as my mother lives, I don’t intend to die any soon
If I die, she will be sad
And don’t plan to kill myself
At least not in a way that make people think that I kill myself
Everything,Life,people

I swear to god I will never, ever kill myself. But swearing is fragile as a twined rope, of course - withstanding pressure is its job, but it can snap and fray under constant weight. If I do, to be as honest as possible, I will do it on the eighth of whatever month I find fit. Preferably in the fall, I find it quite gorgeous. Eight has always been a perfect number to me, round and simple and even. So I'd like to go on my favourite day, if possible. When I relapse sometimes I do it just eight times, eight lines, so I don't slit my whole thigh. Maybe eight is the answer. Eight minutes, eight seconds. Eight days, months, years. Until I stop. I love her like I love the number eight, but I'm not sure she loves me as she loves the number seven. At the very least, though, we go together in every situation. I find that the best part. There would be nowhere to go without her, it would be so wrong. I don't know if she feels the same way for me, like her world revolves around mine as mine does her. There's no lust in the love, no romance to be found. Just the simple love you find between the sky and it's moon, like a small fish clinging to the sea. Maybe it's a pack of eight fish, maybe they swim in the same formation of the cuts on my thigh. Maybe there's a matching set of birds, or the stars I know she likes. Maybe they make a constellation of a fox for her. She's the reason I stay, honestly. Not the only one, maybe, but the largest. I don't mean to say it to keep her around forever if she wouldn't want to, of course. I say it because she's what matters to me. She's like the number eight, or a small fish, or the moon, or the color I see when I close my eyes. She's everywhere, everything, and I do quite hope she stays that way forever.  She's the hesitance in the blade, and the pause in the train of thought rapidly barrelling off a cliff. I wouldn't be where I am today without her. The number eight makes me happy, fish make me happy, coyotes make me happy, the color red makes me happy. She makes me happier than all of that. She isn't any of it, not at all. She's the number 7 and foxes and the color blue - and she's perfect. Maybe I won't do it on the eighth. Maybe I won't do it at all.

I work with someone for whom I'm developing feelings.  This makes me feel guilty and confused; I am in a committed (and otherwise happy) relationship and have no intention of leaving my wonderful partner, yet these feelings that I have are ones whose existence I cannot deny.  I work in a very challenging and trauma-laden field (juvenile corrections); this person with whom I work is someone who endures the same workplace hardships and inherently understands the baggage from said work.  So, I get why we might "bond", so to speak----we're both in the thick of it.  But....we also have a lot in common outside of our chosen profession which all adds up to having a 'work crush' that I fear will grow outside the "normal" bounds of chemistry.  I feel ashamed for even having these feelings even though they aren't willful.  I don't plan on acting on these feelings; my partner is loving, kind and the best person I've ever had alongside me in life.  I guess I just don't understand how two things can be true----I can love my partner and have a 'crush' on someone at work.  I have intellectualized the hell out of this situation (both first responders, both spending time together at work, etc), but it still doesn't ease the reality I face: I like him.  A lot.

I keep getting reminded of what is really going on with the relationship between me and my friends, but ever since another friend pointed out why saying "So that's why they preferred to be with each other." Instantly, I am left dumbfounded by the reality that I thought was real.

At every trip or interaction between the three of us, there was always this space between me and the two of them. I would feel left out, and everyone around me would notice, asking if I was jealous, because I was always left wondering on my own. I always just shook my head and came up with an excuse, because they, too, had me thinking that maybe there was a reason why they wouldn't include me in their conversations.

And when a friend said "that's why" to me, it all clicked. I was the dumbest among us, third to both of them. Always the slowest to solve an equation, they were used to compute in just seconds. I felt hurt.

I felt as if people were only fond of me because I was 'smart', because I had gone to countless extracurriculars: journalism, math competitions, art projects, etc. It's as if they only sought trade when they needed something from me, but all I wanted was for them to acknowledge me aside from my achievements. To see me for me.

Sure, being someone who knew a lot of things had many perks, yet it had twice the number of cons. Constant dependence, expectations, and intimidation. Am I only limited to the things I am capable of doing?

Should I be content with the mistreatment I receive because of my hobbies? I was a person who always voiced out their thoughts out loud, but this feeling of being left out is one of the things I can't seem to advocate for, I can't defend myself, because when it does happen, I do nothing, I leave them be.

Yet, should I be ashamed of my feelings regardless of their significance?

Most of the time, every day, I feel generally okay. But there are times, especially at night, where I feel an overwhelming loneliness. There's nobody I can talk to about it who either cares or hasn't heard it already. As the new year and my birthday approach, I lay here wondering if I'm worthy of it. By "it" I mean kindness, love, hope, even just the general interest and concern of others. I have trouble truly trusting others due to past experiences. This, plus my naturally introverted personality and my autism diagnosis, makes building relationships of any form extremely difficult. Romantic relationships are an especially touchy subject. There are nights where I'm up late wondering what's wrong with me. Am I ugly, inside and/or out? Do I just not try hard enough? Is it my fault my past relationships failed? Why is it so hard for me to meet someone new who likes me? Why does everyone walk away before trying to get to know me? It saddens me to no end when people lead me to believe they're interested in me, only to ignore or walk away from me soon after. I'm especially conscious of how I treat people, yet I fail at every opportunity to make real connections. It's just so hard. I don't know what I'm doing wrong. I try so hard, and it's just not working. Not a week goes by where I wonder if it's worth the struggle. Not a week goes by without me wanting to disappear at some point. There's only so much that medicine and counseling can do. I hate myself, and I hate this world of mine. I want to disappear, I want it to be as if I never existed. Nobody would be sad I'm gone, and I wouldn't have to suffer this crushing loneliness. I need a hug, not from a family member, but from a peer. Someone whose love I earned. But I know I won't get one. Everyone who have given me one? They have walked away. Some even hate me now, and I deserve it. I just want everything to stop, I don't want to feel like this anymore. I need someone, but nobody will come.

I don’t know why I’m here tbh, this seems stupid but I’ll give it a shot anyways, to explain my story it seems kinda dumb but I feel like I can’t let it go and it just follows me till this day, to start explaining it was when Covid-19 came around and got everyone sick and face masks came around, I thought to myself when people didn’t see my face I looked better so I started to always wearing one even freaking out my class mates and even my parents after a bit but they just thought I was scared of the virus so they didn’t question it but I was actually scared of people seeing how I looked like I didn’t want them to judge me from my face I was just scared they wouldn’t accept me because of how I looked it came to a point where a girl asked me out beautiful girl, I didn’t know why she asked me out but I went with it… it was the stupidest thing I ever did because she was actually getting to me I loved her a lot, like a lot and I was embarrassed showing her my affection and I knew she didn’t like that so I decided, that my best course of action was to skip school with her and of course I got caught by the school and my parents but it was the first time I could actually hang out with her even if it was a lil awkward I loved spending time wit her I loved the way she dressed the way she smelt, I know I sound like a weirdo saying that but it’s true.. I did truly admire her a lot and she tried her best to love me and I can tell I knew she loved me so much maybe a bit more then I loved her.. then I started to realize she never saw my face without a mask and the guilt just sank in I started to not talk to her because I didn’t want to show my face I was worried she wasn’t gonna love me I was scared I just stopped responding to her and got distant and that’s when I realized I miss talking to her but I also didn’t want her to be grossed out or anything so I just decided to not talk to her and didn’t respond to her questions on why I had a mask on I felt so guilty and I just couldn’t continue with this guilt but I did… and eventually she had enough with me being selfish and broke up and I knew deep down it was the best thing since if I can’t even show my face why should she love me? She doesn’t know how I look I don’t even talk much about myself why does she love me so much…. I tried to get her back but she didn’t want to hear it and forever I will be guilty and angry about what I never did I’m so sorry Mallory I wasn’t a good boyfriend I didn’t deserve you to begin with I’m deeply sorry even though you will never come across this message I hope you find someone way better then me and could actually show affection and love you for who u are…
