I feel like I've been sexualizing myself from since I was a child, but I don't know if it's my fault or If I should blame someone else. 

(In kindergarden I was really into playing "kiss jacket" (If that's the correct translation), but I don't know if it was sexual or that inappropriate).

My earliest memories of this are from when I was around 7-8 years old. I remember my cousin (that is the same age as me), getting into watching these videos of women in bikinis that can be found on YouTube. I remember him showing me some videos (that were nothing too explicit) and that I was really confused by what was so enthralling about them. Next thing I know, he's convincing me to 'try something' with him. We're at my grandmas house, quiet in a room, naked, him laying on top of me on the floor on a mattress. He's inside of me, skin to skin. I don't know what to feel and I'm mostly confused about what's happening, but he looks like he knows what he's doing, even though he doesn't. He's not moving, just laying there. I can remember this like I'm still there in that room. This happens a few times more before I start saying 'no' to when he was trying to convince me. 

I also remember that once after we had done it with my cousin and I went to school the next day, I'm telling my friends, almost bragging and joking about it. They look disgusted and grossed out, some are laughing at me. When my cousin finds out he's embarrased but angry with me, telling me that I'm not supposed to tell anyone. I was confused and didn't know why it was wrong. Some huge rumors began behind my back, and I believe they're still going on (I'm 15 now).

Probably half a year after that, I get caught by my parents watching 'porn' on my grandparents' tablet. I remember my father asking me where have I found that kind of stuff, me crying in my bed, under the covers from shame. I eventually whisper to him what me and my cousin had been up to, and he leaves the room without another word. We have never spoken about it again, but since then I have not been allowed to sleep in the same room as my cousin. 

There's some singular memories of myself humping like stuffed animals, cuddling and having imaginary "sex" with them. I don't know if that's normal child sexual growth behaviour, but yes, I did that.

A few years go on, I'm 10 now. My cousins best friend (also 10) has taken an interest in me, and I suppose he's been watching those videos too. He started whispering on my ear, touching me more often. I'm uncomfortable but I don't know how to get him to stop. I remember him once, with my cousin in the room, trying to force me to take off my clothes so he could take pictures of my body and send them around. My cousin was luckily also uncomfortable, but didn't help me push the boy off of me. He didn't get my clothes off. I also remember being on floorball practice, wearing a dark blue tank top. He's at the same practice. He walks up to me and yanks my top up so my whole chest is exposed and he laughs. I feel like crying and I can still live trough the memory. Eventually I snap, but it was at school. He's walking by and whispering in my ear again. I put my foot on the ground so he stumbles on it, falling on the floor, he's crying and I'm crying, the teacher scolding me until I tell him what the boy has been doing. I just want to vent out my feelings on my recent issue. 

To start off, I am currently playing an online game where I met a guy who, at first, seemed cold toward me, but when we slowly get to know each other, we became close.

Now, things happened in my life throughout our years of friendship that ended up with me always on the survival mode. Losing a job and having to go through the process of looking for a work from home setup meant that I was riddled with anxiety almost every day, and I was already filled with anxiety issues throughout my life. Basically, I'm a self-deprecating person due to experiencing various issues in my life, but that's besides the issue I'm having at the moment.

You see, this guy that I met has been for the most part, a wonderful guy. Those years of being with him has me developing romantic feelings to him. And, because we're practically joined at the hip, people started asking me if we're in an actual relationship.

Now, I've noticed that whenever I tell this to the guy, he seemed to avoid the topic entirely. At first I thought it was just him being shy, with him having similar personalities with me (introvert) but, when someone asked, once more, if we're dating, I finally comfronted the guy about it.

As it turns out, he doesn't reciprocate my feelings. 

While this is fine in itself, how he "cared" about me is what pains me the most.

Because I had been down in the dumps, he somehow wanted to keep me around, and in an attempt to make me happy, I was strung up to believe that he will return my romantic feelings.

He has basically become my pillar at that point, and he's very important in my life. He became my main motivation to keep going, and it stings to discover that he baited me into thinking that he can return my feelings is because he doesn't want me to be even more miserable than I already am.

Now i'm being an idiot because I miss him already. Waiting for his messages every time I wake up and sleep has become a routine to the both of us that it's so hard to try and convince myself that at the end of the day, we're just friends.

It really sucks when I finally found the person whom I want to possibly spend my life with, only to be told that I was only cared for to prevent self-destruction. It hurts a lot.

I feel so angry and powerless about my health. I want to lose weight for the sake of my health and to relieve some of the pain of my fibromyalgia by putting less strain on my body. But nothing works! I exercise more, and I don't lose any weight. I eat less, I don't lose any weight. I have PCOS, but I can't follow the PCOS diet because I also have gastroparesis. The PCOS diet says to reduce carbs and increase fiber. The gastroparesis diet says to reduce fiber and stick to easy to digest foods; many of which are sugar and carbs. If I follow the PCOS diet, I get really sick. Thats actually how my first gastroparesis flare up happened which lead to me getting diagnosed. I had been following the PCOS diet after getting diagnosed with that, and I started to get really sick, to the point where I could barely hold any food down. Even then I didn't lose weight. Even when I could barely eat, and couldn't digest what I did, I couldn't lose weight. Nothing I do works. I even thought about asking about something like Ozempic... but it turns out, that can cause gastroparesis as a side effect. The last thing I need is to make my gastroparesis worse. 

It just seems like it's impossible for me. I try to exercise, but I can only due low impact exercise because of my fibromyalgia. Even that is extremely painful at the time, but it's good for my fibromyalgia, so I do it anyway. But even when I'm trying to do everything right, my body is constantly fighting against every effort I make.

I'm just so tired of being chronically ill and in pain all the time. I'm tired of people acting like everybody can just lose weight and it's just a matter of laziness or not trying hard enough. I'm in constant pain and I'm still trying. It just doesn't work. Nothing works. I don't know why I even bother to try at this point, but I just want to do SOMETHING to reduce my pain levels and improve the quality of my life.

I'm so tired of it. I just wish I got to live a normal life instead of being multiply-disabled. I wish things didn't hurt so much. I wish things were easier. I'm tired of trying so hard and getting nowhere.

I'm so fed up with this life, I don't even know what to do anymore. I feel like the more I chose to stay, the more fucked up I get. I'm so tired of being a human—of living, in general. God, I'm so exhausted deep in my bones, I carry this heavy body of mine everyday when all I wish is to rest.
